Add a SeedsDepsProvider seam to SpawnRunInput so tests can inject a recording spawn (matches the existing burrowClientPool seam).

steps.title: Consolidate cron tick path onto updateExtensions
steps.description: In src/triggers/tick.ts:184, replace the standalone `clearScheduledFor` write with a single `updateExtensions` call that merges {scheduledFor: null, lastScheduledRun: runId, lastRunId: runId, lastRunAt: now, role: agent.name, trigger: 'cron'} — one shell-out instead of two, idempotent because seeds shallow-merge accepts the same payload twice. Keep the pl-2f15 risk-4 ordering (triggers.last_fired_at stamps BEFORE this call). Update src/triggers/seeds-extension.test.ts assertions and add a case verifying the merged keys.

steps.title: Surface seedId on Run API + minimal RunDetail back-link
steps.description: Add `seedId: string | null` to src/ui/src/api/types.ts Run shape, the server response composer, and src/ui/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx header — show a `↩ seeds-xxxx` link next to the existing fields when present. No issues page work here (that's R-04). Goal is just to make the seed binding observable in the UI so an operator inspecting a cron run sees the source seed without grepping events.

steps.title: Acceptance scenario: seeds-extensions-roundtrip
steps.description: Add scripts/acceptance/scenarios/seeds-extensions-roundtrip.ts. Create a project with a seed, POST /runs with `seedId` for the manual path, wait for the run to enter `running`, then `sd show <seed> --format json` and assert extensions.{lastRunId, lastRunAt, role, trigger:'manual'}. Repeat against a cron-triggered run via the triggers HTTP surface and assert extensions.scheduledFor === null AND lastScheduledRun === runId AND lastRunId === runId AND trigger:'cron'. Idempotent + self-cleaning per scripts/acceptance conventions.

steps.title: Flip R-01 status in ROADMAP.md + record mulch convention
steps.description: Edit ROADMAP.md:47 from `[partially shipped]` to `[shipped — producer side; consumer-side reads ride with R-04]`. Update the cross-repo dashboard row (line 1501) if needed; update sequencing entry at line 1539 to reflect closure. `ml record runs convention --description 'spawnRun writes warren-namespaced seed extensions {role,trigger,lastRunId,lastRunAt} via src/seeds-cli/updateExtensions; failures degrade to system events, never abort the run' --evidence-seeds warren-5a9c`. Then `ml sync && sd sync && git push`.

risks: sd not on PATH inside burrow workers: warren already shells out via SpawnFn elsewhere, but acceptance must verify the host-side path used by warren-the-supervisor, not the inside-burrow path. Mitigate by reusing the SeedsCliDeps shape from triggers, which is already exercised in production.

Race between operator-edits to `sd update --extensions` and warren's post-dispatch write: seeds uses shallow-merge so concurrent writes to different keys are safe; same-key writes are last-write-wins. Acceptable for denormalized pointer metadata.
seed_id added to runs but never written for legacy runs: nullable column + UI back-link gated on `seedId !== null` keeps backward compat. No backfill needed.

lastRunAt semantics drift (started-at vs finished-at): convention in ROADMAP.md:99 reads as start-time; reap-side rewrite would be a separate decision. Document the choice in the schema doc-comment so R-04 reads consistently.

updateExtensions failure leaving a stale seed: surface as a system event and a (future) `warren doctor` check that diffs runs.seed_id against seed.extensions.lastRunId. Filed as a follow-up seed if not in scope.

Trigger-string proliferation (manual / manual-trigger / cron / webhook / comment): zod enum locks it down; src/server/handlers.ts:354 currently uses `manual-trigger` and src/runs/spawn.ts:214 uses `manual`. Reconcile in step 2's schema.
acceptance: src/seeds-cli/ module exports updateExtensions + listScheduledSeeds + clearScheduledFor; src/triggers/seeds-extension.ts either deleted or a thin re-export. All existing trigger tests pass.
POST /runs accepts optional seedId; runs row persists seed_id; SQLite + Postgres migrations both land and acceptance scenario 19 still green.
After a manual POST /runs with seedId, `sd show <seed> --format json` shows extensions.{role,trigger:'manual',lastRunId,lastRunAt}.
After a cron fire, the seed's extensions show {scheduledFor:null, lastScheduledRun, lastRunId, lastRunAt, role, trigger:'cron'} — written via a single sd update.
Extension write failure emits a `seeds_extension_write_failed` system event on the run and does NOT abort the run (verified by test that injects a failing SpawnFn).
RunDetail UI shows a seed back-link when seedId is present.
New acceptance scenario `seeds-extensions-roundtrip` lives in scripts/acceptance/scenarios/ and is idempotent.
Quality gates pass: `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` clean, no warnings.
ROADMAP.md R-01 status updated; mulch convention recorded under `runs` domain; `sd sync && ml sync && git push` shows up-to-date with origin.

name: R-01 consumer-side: seeds extensions in spawn/UI

sections.context:
Today warren's agent registry has two tiers: built-ins shipped in `src/registry/builtins/` (claude-code, sapling, pi) and a single shared canopy library loaded from `CANOPY_REPO_URL`. There is no tier for roles that should travel with one project — e.g. a refactor-bot tuned to one codebase's conventions. Forcing project-specific roles into a shared library couples unrelated projects' role definitions and makes ad-hoc role iteration a five-step round-trip (clone canopy repo → write prompt → push → set env → refresh). R-03 closes the gap by scanning each project's `<projectPath>/.canopy/` as a third tier with precedence project > library > built-in, stamped on each agent row so the UI can show provenance. It is a load-bearing prerequisite for R-05 (in-UI roles editor) — without per-project storage there is nowhere safe for the editor to write — and rounds out R-04's roles picker, which surfaces a project tier with a `project:<id>` source label.

Key code shape today: `src/registry/canopy.ts` (the `cn` facade, cwd hardcoded to the library clone), `src/registry/refresh.ts` (drives `cn list` + `cn render` + `agents.upsert`), `src/registry/builtins/index.ts` (boot-time seeding), `src/db/repos/agents.ts` (keyed by `name` globally; `runs.agent_name` FK), `src/runs/spawn.ts:149` (`repos.agents.require(agentName)` — projectId-unaware), `src/server/handlers.ts` (`withAgentSource` decorates rows; `refreshAgents` is `POST /agents/refresh`). The agents table is a cache (`recoveryHint: "POST /agents/refresh to re-discover"`), which is what makes the schema change in step 1 viable.

Decisions locked before submission (2026-05-15): (1) `POST /agents/refresh` iterates all projects automatically after the library pass; (2) cross-tier inheritance (per-project `extends:` library/built-in) is deferred to a follow-up — V2 ships single-tier inheritance only; (3) on-disk render cache under `<projectPath>/.canopy/.rendered/` is deferred to a follow-up; (4) uniqueness is enforced at the DB layer via a composite unique on `(project_id, name)` plus a partial unique on `(name) WHERE project_id IS NULL`, with a synthetic rowid PK replacing today's `name` PK.
sections.approach:
Persist the per-project tier in the existing `agents` table by adding a nullable `project_id` column. Drop the single-column `name` primary key in favor of a synthetic rowid PK; enforce identity with two indexes: composite unique on `(project_id, name)` and partial unique on `(name) WHERE project_id IS NULL` (the latter guarantees one global row per name even though sqlite treats NULL as distinct). `NULL` means global tier (built-in or library); a non-null `project_id` means project-scoped. Matches the ROADMAP sketch (`POST /agents/refresh ... stamps the source onto each agents row (source: builtin | library | project:<projectId>)`).

The `runs.agent_name → agents.name` FK becomes incompatible with composite identity, so drop it. The agents table is already a soft cache that documents itself as 'refresh to re-discover'; spawn looks up `(name, projectId)` then falls back to `(name, NULL)` at the application layer.

For rendering, parameterize `CanopyClient`'s cwd so the same facade drives both the library clone and per-project `.canopy/` directories. Add `refreshProjectAgents(projectId)` next to `refreshAgentRegistry`. `POST /agents/refresh` does library + every project in one call (per-project errors collected, not fatal). `POST /projects/:id/agents/refresh` is the targeted path R-04/R-05 will call from the UI.

Source provenance: stamp `renderedJson.frontmatter.source = 'project:<projectId>'` for project-tier rows during refresh, the same way built-ins set `'builtin'` today. Widen `readAgentSource()` and the `AgentSource` type. Spawn-time freezing into `runs.rendered_agent_json` is unchanged.

Two ROADMAP open questions are deferred to follow-up seeds filed in step 10: cross-tier inheritance, and the on-disk rendered cache.

alternatives.name: Resolve per-project agents at spawn time only; don't persist
alternatives.rejected_because: Lighter, but loses the listing surface R-04's roles tab and R-05's editor need. Also makes `GET /agents` lie about what's available.

alternatives.name: Namespace the name (`<projectId>:<role>`) and keep agents PK as `name`
alternatives.rejected_because: Avoids the schema migration but pushes the project-scope concept into a string convention the UI has to parse — ugly and brittle, and the FK problem returns once you want clean listing.

alternatives.name: Composite PK + update runs.agent_name to a composite FK
alternatives.rejected_because: Strictly safer than dropping the FK, but rippling the change into `runs.agentName` (and every existing call site, plus both dialects) is far more invasive than the cache it would guard. The 'soft cache' framing of the agents table is the right precedent.

alternatives.name: Empty-string sentinel for global tier in a composite PK
alternatives.rejected_because: Removes the NULL-distinct problem but requires every read site to remember the sentinel, and the schema lies about identity (an empty string isn't 'no project'). Partial unique index is the right DB-layer expression.

steps.title: Schema migration: add agents.project_id (nullable), drop name PK, add composite + partial unique indexes, drop runs.agent_name FK
steps.description: In `src/db/schema/sqlite.ts` and `src/db/schema/postgres.ts`, on the `agents` table: replace `name TEXT PRIMARY KEY` with `id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT` (sqlite) / `id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY` (postgres), add `project_id TEXT NULL`, and declare two indexes via drizzle's index builders — a composite unique on `(project_id, name)` and a partial unique on `(name) WHERE project_id IS NULL`. On `runs` in both dialects, remove the `.references(() => agents.name)` clause from `agent_name` (column stays `TEXT NOT NULL`, no FK). Write paired migrations: `src/db/migrations/0010_add_agents_project_id.sql` (sqlite — table rebuild via `ALTER TABLE agents RENAME TO __agents_old`, `CREATE TABLE agents (...)`, `INSERT INTO agents (name, rendered_json, registered_at, last_refreshed) SELECT ...`, `DROP TABLE __agents_old`, then `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX agents_project_name ON agents(project_id, name)` and `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX agents_name_global ON agents(name) WHERE project_id IS NULL`; the runs FK drop also requires a runs rebuild because sqlite cannot drop FKs in place) and `src/db/migrations/postgres/0003_add_agents_project_id.sql` (postgres — `ALTER TABLE agents DROP CONSTRAINT agents_pkey, ADD COLUMN id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, ADD COLUMN project_id TEXT NULL`, `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX agents_project_name ON agents(project_id, name)`, `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX agents_name_global ON agents(name) WHERE project_id IS NULL`, `ALTER TABLE runs DROP CONSTRAINT runs_agent_name_agents_name_fk`). Update `src/db/schema/drift.test.ts` so the cross-dialect comparison continues to pass after the column add, the PK change, and the FK removal — adjust the FK assertion to expect zero FKs from `runs.agent_name` and assert the new indexes exist in both dialects. Existing rows are preserved as global-tier (`project_id` stays NULL).

steps.title: Widen AgentSource + source-stamping helpers
steps.description: In `src/registry/builtins/index.ts`, widen `AgentSource` to `'builtin' | 'library' | `project:${string}``. Update `readAgentSource()` to return the `project:<projectId>` literal when `frontmatter.source` starts with `project:`. Add a `stampProjectSource(definition, projectId)` helper that returns a new `AgentDefinition` with `frontmatter.source = `project:${projectId}``. Built-ins keep `frontmatter.source = 'builtin'`; library rows keep the absent-source → `'library'` fallback. Add unit tests in `src/registry/builtins/builtins.test.ts` for the project literal round-trip.

steps.title: Extend AgentsRepo with scope-aware reads + writes
steps.description: In `src/db/repos/agents.ts`, change every query to filter by `projectId` (undefined or null → `project_id IS NULL`; string → `project_id = ?`). New methods: `upsert({ name, projectId?, renderedJson })`, `getScoped({ name, projectId })`, `requireScoped({ name, projectId })`, `listAll({ projectId? })` where `undefined` returns all tiers, `null` returns global-only, and a string returns that project's tier only, `deleteScoped({ name, projectId })`. Add `requireForProject(name, projectId)` that tries `(name, projectId)` then `(name, NULL)` — this is the resolver `spawnRun` calls. Keep the existing zero-arg `listAll()` overload working by treating it as 'all tiers'. Cover same-name collision across two projects, global-tier fallback, and scoped delete leaving other tiers intact in `src/db/repos/agents.test.ts`.

steps.title: Parameterize CanopyClient cwd; add forProjectPath() factory
steps.description: In `src/registry/canopy.ts`, change `CanopyClient.invoke()` to accept an optional `cwd` per call (default stays `this.config.localDir`). Add `listAgents({ cwd? })` and `renderAgent(name, { cwd? })` signatures. Add a static `CanopyClient.forProjectPath(projectPath, baseConfig)` factory that returns a client whose default cwd is `<projectPath>/.canopy/`, sharing the same `cnBinary` + spawn + timeout settings. Update `canopy.test.ts` cwd assertions. Wire format and response shapes are unchanged.

steps.title: Add refreshProjectAgents() to scan + render a project's .canopy/
steps.description: New function in `src/registry/refresh.ts` mirroring `refreshAgentRegistry` scoped to one project: takes `{ projectId, projectPath, client, agents, prune?, now? }`. Probe `<projectPath>/.canopy/` with `existsSync`; if absent, return `{ registered: [], skipped: [], removed: [] }` (not an error). Otherwise, list via `cn list --tag agent --json` in the project cwd, render each, call `stampProjectSource(def, projectId)`, and `agents.upsert({ name, projectId, renderedJson })`. Per-agent errors collect into `skipped` (same posture as the library refresh). When `prune === true`, prune only rows where `project_id = projectId`. Add a unit test that uses an injected spawn to fake `cn`.

steps.title: HTTP surface: per-project refresh route + project filter on list/get
steps.description: In `src/server/handlers.ts`: add `refreshProjectAgentsHandler` for `POST /projects/:id/agents/refresh` — resolves the project, instantiates `CanopyClient.forProjectPath(project.localPath, ...)`, calls `refreshProjectAgents`, returns `{ projectId, registered, skipped, removed }`. Extend `refreshAgents` (the global handler) to also iterate every project after the library refresh, returning a response that includes the existing top-level `{ clone, registered, skipped, removed }` plus a new `projects: [{ projectId, registered, skipped, removed }]` array; per-project errors are collected, never fatal. Extend `listAgents` to accept `?projectId=<id>` — when set, return the global tier UNIONed with that project's tier; when absent, return the global tier only. Extend `getAgent` (`GET /agents/:name`) to accept `?projectId=` and use the same fallback resolution. Wire the new route in the handler array next to `/agents/refresh`. Update `src/server/types.ts` response shapes.

steps.title: Make spawn prefer project tier with fallback to global
steps.description: In `src/runs/spawn.ts` change `const agentRow = await input.repos.agents.require(input.agentName)` to `await input.repos.agents.requireForProject(input.agentName, input.projectId)`. No other spawn changes — `renderedAgentJson` already freezes whatever envelope it received, so a project-scoped agent's `frontmatter.source = 'project:<id>'` rides into the run row unchanged. Update `src/runs/spawn.test.ts` with a scenario where the same name resolves project-scoped for project A and global for project B.

steps.title: UI surface: client projectId filter + provenance badge on Agents/NewRun
steps.description: In `src/ui/src/api/client.ts`, widen `agents.list()` to accept `{ projectId? }` and propagate as a query param; widen the `AgentRow.source` type to include `project:${string}`. In `src/ui/src/pages/Agents.tsx`, render a small badge for project-tier rows showing the project name (look up via the existing projects list). In `src/ui/src/pages/NewRun.tsx`, when a project is selected, call `agents.list({ projectId })` so project-scoped roles surface in the role picker; when no project is selected, behavior is unchanged. Add a 'Refresh project agents' affordance on the Agents page that POSTs `/projects/:id/agents/refresh`. This step delivers the minimum data + provenance surface R-04/R-05 will consume; it does not build R-04's full project-scoped nav or R-05's editor.

steps.title: Acceptance scenario: project tier roundtrip
steps.description: Add `scripts/acceptance/scenarios/22-canopy-project-tier.ts` (next slot after `21-claude-code-cost-smoke.ts`). Scenario: register a project whose repo has a committed `.canopy/<role>.md`, call `POST /projects/:id/agents/refresh`, assert `GET /agents?projectId=<id>` returns the project-tier row with `source: 'project:<id>'`, dispatch a run against that role name with the same project, wait for the run to reach a stable state, assert `runs.rendered_agent_json.frontmatter.source === 'project:<id>'`. Idempotent + self-cleaning per existing scenario conventions. Wire the new scenario id into the harness file that lists them.

steps.title: Docs + follow-up seeds
steps.description: Update `SPEC.md` §8.1 (agents) to document the three-tier precedence, the `project_id` column, the `source: 'project:<id>'` provenance, and the new `POST /projects/:id/agents/refresh` route. Mark R-03 `[shipped]` in `ROADMAP.md` with the plan id `pl-fef5`, and add an entry under `## Recently shipped`. Add a CHANGELOG entry under the next minor version. File two follow-up seeds: (a) 'Cross-tier inheritance: per-project canopy roles `extends:` library/built-in parents' (closing the ROADMAP R-03 open question; will require merging library prompts into the project's canopy index at refresh time); (b) 'On-disk rendered cache under `<projectPath>/.canopy/.rendered/` for `cn emit` compatibility' (pairs naturally with R-05's in-UI editor save path).

steps.title: Quality gates + mulch record + close
steps.description: Run `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck`. Run the new acceptance scenario against a live warren+burrow stack. Run `ml learn`, then `ml record warren --type decision --description 'agents table widened to (project_id, name) with rowid PK and partial unique on (name) WHERE project_id IS NULL; runs.agent_name FK dropped — agents are a soft cache, FK on a refreshable cache was load-bearing for nothing'` and `ml record warren --type pattern --description 'CanopyClient.forProjectPath() is the project-scoped factory; same facade drives library + project tier'`. Close child seeds, close warren-2842, `sd sync && ml sync && git push`.

risks: Dropping `runs.agent_name → agents.name` FK in sqlite requires a runs table rebuild (sqlite cannot drop FKs in place). The agents column-add + PK change also requires a rebuild. Both rebuilds in one migration, preserving rows; the `migrations.test.ts` parity check has to be green on both dialects before merging.

Sqlite treats NULL as distinct in unique indexes, so the composite `(project_id, name)` alone doesn't prevent two global rows for the same name. The partial unique `(name) WHERE project_id IS NULL` is the load-bearing index — confirm drizzle emits the `WHERE` clause for both sqlite and postgres, or write it raw in the migration.

Source-string parsing — anything that does `source === 'library'` instead of `source !== 'builtin'` breaks for project-tier rows. Grep callers of `readAgentSource` and the UI's source field; widen to `startsWith('project:')` where needed.

Per-project `cn list/render` runs in the project's worktree. A half-finished `.canopy/` write (missing index, malformed frontmatter) must skip gracefully — never let one project's bad prompt take down the global refresh. Mirror the library refresh's per-agent error collection.
Cross-tier inheritance is deferred but the ROADMAP says 'yes — that's the whole point.' The follow-up seed filed in step 10 must be visible to R-05 consumers so they don't assume V2 supports it.
acceptance: Migration applies cleanly on a sqlite DB with existing agent rows and on a postgres DB; `bun test` (including `migrations.test.ts`) is green on both dialects.
`agents` rows are addressable by `(name, projectId)` with `projectId` NULL for built-in/library and a concrete project id for project-tier rows. Partial unique index prevents duplicate global rows.
`POST /agents/refresh` refreshes the library AND every project's `.canopy/` in one call; per-project errors are collected and returned, never fatal.
`POST /projects/:id/agents/refresh` refreshes one project's tier and returns `{ projectId, registered, skipped, removed }`.
`GET /agents?projectId=<id>` returns global ∪ that project's tier; `GET /agents` (no filter) returns global only. `GET /agents/:name?projectId=<id>` resolves project-first with global fallback.

`POST /runs` with a name present in both tiers picks the project-tier row when `projectId` matches; falls back to global otherwise. `runs.rendered_agent_json.frontmatter.source` reflects the chosen tier.
Acceptance scenario `22-canopy-project-tier.ts` passes against a live warren+burrow stack.
UI shows a project-tier badge on the Agents page and surfaces project-scoped roles in NewRun's role picker when a project is selected.
`bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` clean — Biome warnings count as failures per the repo gate.
ROADMAP R-03 marked `[shipped]` with plan id `pl-fef5`; SPEC §8.1 updated; CHANGELOG entry added; two follow-up seeds filed (cross-tier inheritance, on-disk rendered cache).

sections.context: R-19 (pl-2c59) shipped the per-run preview-environment infrastructure: schema, port allocator, reap-time launcher, host proxy + signed cookie, eviction worker, UI affordance, acceptance scenario, docs. Dogfooding the result against jayminwest.com (Next.js 14.2.24) on the warren-deployed (postgres) fly instance surfaced five distinct gaps that don't fit into any single child of pl-2c59 but together prevent a fresh-install operator from getting working previews for real-world SPA projects. Two are operator-visible bugs (vestigial sqlite-only teardown gate; unbounded probe fetch causing a 6m47s readiness wait against a 5m timeout); two are sidecar/launcher structural fixes (Next.js dev server binds to localhost-only by default, requiring a project-side `-H` workaround; install and dev-server bind share one timeout and one failure reason); one is the unlock that closes the loop (path-mode HTML loads but every root-relative asset hits warren's SPA fallback, blanking the preview). Without these, the R-19 ROADMAP claim that path mode is the zero-DNS default is technically true but operationally false for any modern SPA framework.
sections.approach: Adopt the five existing follow-up seeds into one feature plan and land them in ascending blast radius. Bug fixes ship first (warren-a743 is a one-line gate removal; warren-33eb adds an AbortController to one fetch call) so dogfooding stops being painful. Sidecar/launcher refactors land next (warren-79b2 hybridizes env + flag injection per CLI; warren-d9e7 splits `setup` from `command` in the schema, adding setup_failed/setup_timeout failure reasons). The SPA path-mode unlock (warren-63e1) lands last because it depends on the cleaner launcher foundation and is the largest behavior change (referer-routed asset proxying + per-run cookie name with Path=/ + abs-path HTML rewriter). Each step is an existing open seed adopted into this plan via `sd plan adopt --step <i>`; no new child seeds are spawned. Alternatives like batching the bugs into a separate plan or skipping the plan and using `sd dep add` were rejected because the dogfood-driven arc reads as a single coherent unit and one plan revision is easier for reviewers to trace.

steps.title: Drop sqlite-only gate in /runs/:id/preview/teardown handler (warren-a743)
Add per-call AbortController timeout to preview readiness probe fetch (warren-33eb)
Inject HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0 (+HOST/PORT) and emit Next.js -H flag in sidecar env (warren-79b2)
Split `setup` from `command` in preview schema for separate timeout + eager-boot prep (warren-d9e7)
Path-mode out-of-the-box for SPAs: referer routing + per-run cookie + abs-path HTML rewrite (warren-63e1)

acceptance: All five child seeds (warren-a743, warren-33eb, warren-79b2, warren-d9e7, warren-63e1) are adopted into this plan at step indices 1–5 respectively.
Each child closes with its own per-seed acceptance criteria intact (see individual seed descriptions).
Operator on the warren-deployed (postgres) instance can click Teardown on a live preview and get a 200, not a 503 (warren-a743).
Preview launcher's readiness loop can never block past `readiness_timeout` due to a single hung probe call (warren-33eb).
A Next.js 14.x project under `.warren/preview.yaml` boots and serves traffic without any project-side `-H 0.0.0.0` workaround (warren-79b2).
Operators can declare `preview.setup` separately from `preview.command`; install failures surface as `preview_failure_message: setup failed: …` distinct from readiness failures (warren-d9e7).

A fresh-install operator pointing warren at a stock Next.js / Vite / CRA project gets a working preview at `/p/<id>/` with all root-relative assets resolving correctly, no per-project HTML changes (warren-63e1).
`bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` pass after each child lands.

name: Preview reliability & SPA support hardening (post-jayminwest.com dogfood)

sections.context: Plot v1 has shipped from ../plot/ as @os-eco/plot-cli. Warren's primary coordination unit today is the run (orchestrator → worker); the post-V1 direction calls for a shared Plot substrate where humans and agents are peer nodes. warren-000b is the umbrella epic for that shift and is sequenced in three phases: (1) dispatch wiring, (2) PlanRun composition (owned by warren-fcc9), (3) UI shift. This plan decomposes Phase 1 only — the wiring that lets a dispatched run know which Plot it belongs to, lets the agent inside the sandbox read and append to that Plot via the `plot` CLI, and mirrors agent-emitted Plot events into warren's event stream tagged with `plot_id`. No UI changes. Plot integration is gated on the project having a `.plot/` directory, matching the existing seeds/mulch opt-in shape, so projects without Plot are unaffected. The work follows the proven pattern warren already uses for `.mulch/`/`.seeds/`/`.canopy/`/`.pi/` integration — feature-detect on project refresh, opt-in env injection at spawn time, merge workspace artifacts back at reap time.
sections.approach: Mirror the existing mulch/seeds opt-in integration shape end-to-end: (a) add `@os-eco/plot-cli` as a runtime dep pinned in two places (package.json + bun.lock AND Dockerfile global install) per the burrow-cli double-pin lesson recorded in CLAUDE.md — the binary must be on PATH inside the burrow so agents can shell `plot get` and `plot append`; (b) build a typed facade in `src/plot-client/` over the library export (`PlotStore`, `SQLitePlotIndex` from `@os-eco/plot-cli`) so warren's server side never talks to the JSONL/SQLite files directly — same pattern as `src/burrow-client/`; (c) extend the project refresh flow to detect `.plot/` and add the boolean to the project row (parallel to existing mulch/seeds detection); (d) widen the run-create API + DB schema with an optional `plot_id` column, validated on dispatch (reject `plot_id` when project has no `.plot/`); (e) at spawn time, when both gating flags are present, inject `PLOT_ID` + `PLOT_ACTOR=agent:<name>:<run-id>` into the sandbox env via the burrow_up call; (f) immediately after spawn, the server-side facade appends a `run_dispatched` event to the originating Plot using the dispatching user's actor (warren acts on behalf of `user:<handle>` per the seed's write-ACL constraint); (g) at reap time, replay `.plot/pl-*.events.jsonl` deltas from the burrow workspace, fold the agent-emitted `decision_made` / `question_posed` / `artifact_produced` entries into warren's existing event stream tagged with `plot_id`, and merge the JSONL back into the project's `.plot/` the same way `.mulch/` and `.seeds/` are merged today; (h) the facade hard-refuses to construct `intent_edited` / `status_changed` / `attachment_removed` / `question_answered` calls from an agent-actor — Plot's library would reject them anyway, but defending at the warren edge keeps the SPEC §6 ACL legible and gives us a unit test that pins the contract. JSONL-tail-at-reap is chosen over a live socket bridge for V1: deterministic, no new plumbing, and the loss of live UI updates doesn't matter until Phase 3. The library import (not subprocess) is chosen for warren↔Plot the same way it's chosen for warren↔burrow.

alternatives.name: Shell out to the `plot` CLI from the warren server instead of importing the library
alternatives.rejected_because: Warren imports `@os-eco/burrow` as a typed facade rather than shelling `burrow` because subprocess JSON round-trips lose types and add latency on the hot path. Same logic applies to Plot — `@os-eco/plot-cli` exports `PlotStore` + `SQLitePlotIndex` precisely for embedders. The CLI binary is still shipped inside the burrow so agents can shell `plot get` and `plot append` from inside the sandbox; that's a separate surface.

alternatives.name: Live bridge: stream agent-emitted Plot events to warren via a burrow socket sidecar instead of replaying JSONL at reap
alternatives.rejected_because: Would force a new bidirectional channel between burrow and warren just for Plot events. JSONL-tail-at-reap is what `.mulch/` and `.seeds/` already do — same merge primitive, no new plumbing. The cost is no live Plot-event surfacing in the warren UI until reap completes, which is irrelevant in Phase 1 (no UI shift yet) and an additive improvement in Phase 3.

alternatives.name: Auto-detect a 'current Plot' from the project and inject `PLOT_ID` implicitly
alternatives.rejected_because: There is no globally-correct 'current Plot' for a project — a project may host many open Plots concurrently. Implicit selection is a foot-gun (an agent attributes work to the wrong Plot). Explicit `plot_id` on the dispatch API is the same shape mulch/seeds/canopy use for project-binding inputs.

alternatives.name: Silently no-op (drop the env vars) when `plot_id` is supplied but `.plot/` is missing
alternatives.rejected_because: Operator error gets buried — the dispatched run looks normal but the Plot never records the dispatch. Hard-reject at the API surface with a typed error (mirroring how invalid agent or model names already reject) is loud, recoverable, and matches warren's no-half-spawned-state policy.

alternatives.name: Skip the warren-side ACL guard; rely on Plot library to reject humans-only event types from agent actors
alternatives.rejected_because: The seed explicitly says 'warren shouldn't construct them.' A facade-level type narrowing turns this into a compile-time guarantee plus one unit test, instead of a runtime error caught by Plot. Cheap defense in depth; mirrors how `src/burrow-client/` narrows the burrow HTTP surface.

alternatives.name: Postpone DB column on runs; derive `plot_id` from the run's event stream
alternatives.rejected_because: The `plot_id`→run lookup is the hot path for the Phase 3 UI shift and for the Phase 2 PlanRun composition (warren-fcc9 will need to query 'runs for this Plot' cheaply). A nullable indexed column is the obvious shape and the migration is trivial; deferring it just builds technical debt that the next two phases pay for.

alternatives.name: Ship Plot in V1 as a always-on integration (not gated on `.plot/`)
alternatives.rejected_because: Breaks the standalone fresh-install path that warren/CLAUDE.md explicitly preserves: 'a user with a GitHub URL and an Anthropic key can dispatch a run end-to-end with no other tooling.' Gating on `.plot/` keeps Plot strictly additive.

steps.title: Foundation: add @os-eco/plot-cli dependency (package.json + bun.lock + Dockerfile global install per burrow-cli double-pin rule), and add a typed facade at src/plot-client/ over PlotStore + SQLitePlotIndex with a write-ACL narrowing that refuses humans-only event types from agent-actors

Project feature detection: extend src/projects/refresh.ts to detect .plot/ alongside the existing .mulch/.seeds/.canopy/.pi/ probes; surface the boolean on the project row, project types, GET /projects/:id payload, and UI types (read-only; no UI change beyond making the field available)

DB + API surface for run.plot_id: add nullable `plot_id` column to the runs schema with an index, plumb through the runs repo, accept optional `plot_id` on POST /runs, validate at handler-level (reject when project has no .plot/ with a typed error), surface in GET /runs/:id and the event-stream meta

Spawn-time env injection: in src/runs/spawn.ts (and burrow_config plumbing if needed), when both project.hasPlot and run.plot_id are present, inject PLOT_ID=<run.plot_id> and PLOT_ACTOR=agent:<agent-name>:<run-id> into the sandbox environment via the burrow up call; assert the resulting env in spawn.test.ts; verify Dockerfile-installed `plot` binary is on PATH inside the burrow

run_dispatched emission: immediately after a successful spawn with plot_id, append a run_dispatched event to the originating Plot via src/plot-client/ using the dispatching user's actor (user:<handle>) with payload {run_id, agent, model, project}; handle the case where .plot/.index.db doesn't exist yet on first dispatch (best-effort plot rebuild-index, then retry once); failure to append does NOT fail the spawn but is logged as a warren event so operators see it

Reap-time event mirroring + .plot/ merge: in src/runs/reap.ts, replay the burrow workspace's .plot/pl-*.events.jsonl deltas after spawn, mirror agent-emitted decision_made / question_posed / artifact_produced entries into warren's event stream tagged with plot_id, and merge the workspace .plot/ back into the project's persistent .plot/ using the same primitive .mulch and .seeds already use (idempotent, conflict-on-content); update reap.test.ts to cover the round-trip

Write-ACL defensive unit tests in src/plot-client/: assert that the facade refuses to construct intent_edited / status_changed / attachment_removed / question_answered when the configured actor is agent:* (compile-time narrowing where possible, runtime throw otherwise); verify warren code paths never reach the disallowed surface

Acceptance scenario 25 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/25-plot-roundtrip.ts): end-to-end against a real warren+burrow stack — init a project with a .plot/ directory containing one Plot, dispatch a run with plot_id, assert PLOT_ID + PLOT_ACTOR reach the sandbox env (via a tool the test agent runs), assert `plot append` from inside the sandbox lands in .plot/pl-*.events.jsonl post-reap, assert the run_dispatched event is present in the Plot's event log, assert warren's event stream contains the mirrored events tagged with plot_id, and assert dispatching against a project WITHOUT .plot/ is byte-identical to the pre-change behavior (snapshot the events)

SPEC + docs: add a new subsection to warren/SPEC.md ('Plot integration' — data flow, gating, ACL surface, env contract); update warren/README.md and warren/CLAUDE.md feature list to mention Plot as the fifth opt-in bundled feature; add CHANGELOG.md entry; run the full quality trinity (bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck) and the new acceptance scenario before marking the plan done

risks: Double-pin drift on @os-eco/plot-cli: the same pitfall warren has with @os-eco/burrow-cli — pinned in both package.json/bun.lock AND the Dockerfile global install. Step 1 must touch both and the release workflow should fail on mismatch. Easy to bump only one and silently run the wrong version.

Workspace .plot/ merge semantics aren't identical to .mulch/.seeds/: Plot is JSON-per-Plot plus append-only JSONL event logs. Merging two divergent event logs is not commutative if both warren-side and burrow-side appended in the same window. Step 6 must use a content-addressed merge (event ids deduplicate) and reject on conflicting Plot JSON bodies rather than last-writer-wins.

.plot/.index.db rebuild on first dispatch: the SQLite index is gitignored and rebuildable. If a project's first ever Plot dispatch finds no index, step 5's `plot rebuild-index` retry is the documented escape hatch — but if the project has many Plots, the rebuild cost on the dispatch hot path is noticeable. Mitigate by triggering the rebuild during the projects/refresh pass (step 2) instead of lazily at dispatch.

Actor format for non-user dispatch paths: the README documents `user:<handle>` and `agent:<name>[:<run-id>]` only. Cron/scheduler-triggered dispatches (SPEC §11.I) don't have a user actor. Step 5 needs a decision — either a `system:warren` actor (requires Plot library support, check with ../plot/SPEC.md §6) or attribute scheduled runs to the trigger's owner handle. Same risk applies to webhook-triggered runs in V2.

Sandbox PATH for the `plot` binary: warren currently relies on the Dockerfile global install for `burrow`. `Bun.spawn` resolves `./node_modules/.bin/<tool>` before PATH. Inside the burrow, the global install IS the PATH-resolvable copy; verify with the same idiom used in step 4 of the acceptance scenario. If a future change moves to local node_modules resolution for plot CLI, the sandbox will silently lose access.

Live UI gap during Phase 1: JSONL-tail-at-reap means Plot events from a long-running agent don't appear in warren's stream until the run completes. Acceptable for Phase 1 (no Plot UI yet) but worth recording so Phase 3 plans for a live channel from day one rather than discovering the gap during UI build.

ACL guard false confidence: type-narrowing in src/plot-client/ stops warren code from constructing humans-only events, but only the Plot library can enforce it across all consumers. Don't gate the test suite on 'library refuses X' — test that warren's facade refuses to construct X. Otherwise we couple to library internals.

tsc + bun-types duplicate-declaration trap (mx-ef8eab): adding @os-eco/plot-cli pulls another dep tree under node_modules. If plot-cli ships with its own bun-types declaration, the src/ui tsc build may trip on duplicate ImportMeta.hot. Verify build:ui still green after step 1; the existing tsconfig exclude pattern may need extending.

Reap-time merge under concurrent runs: two simultaneous warren runs bound to the same Plot will both write event log deltas. Step 6's merge primitive must be ordered by event timestamp + actor and idempotent on replay, otherwise reaping in the wrong order corrupts the Plot's event log.

Dispatch validation ordering: rejecting `plot_id` when the project has no `.plot/` must happen BEFORE any side effect (no row insert, no burrow up). Failing late leaves a half-spawned run row that reaper has to garbage-collect. Step 3 puts the check before src/runs/spawn.ts is called.
acceptance: package.json + bun.lock + Dockerfile global install all reference the same pinned v1 minor of @os-eco/plot-cli; CI fails on mismatch.
GET /projects/:id includes a `hasPlot` (or equivalent) boolean reflecting `.plot/` presence; projects refresh detects it the same way it detects .mulch/.seeds/.canopy/.pi/.
POST /runs accepts an optional `plot_id`; the run row stores it; GET /runs/:id surfaces it.
POST /runs with `plot_id` against a project that has no `.plot/` returns a typed 4xx error and creates no run row.
A run dispatched with `plot_id` has PLOT_ID=<plot_id> and PLOT_ACTOR=agent:<agent-name>:<run-id> in its sandbox env, verified by an in-sandbox `printenv` snapshot inside scenario 25.
A `run_dispatched` event with actor=user:<handle> and payload {run_id, agent, model, project} appears in the originating Plot's event log within one tick of a successful spawn.
Agent-emitted decision_made / question_posed / artifact_produced events made from inside the sandbox via `plot append` are visible in the project's persistent .plot/pl-*.events.jsonl after reap.
The same events are mirrored into warren's event stream for the run, tagged with `plot_id`, and visible via the existing event-stream endpoint.
Dispatching a run against a project WITHOUT `.plot/` produces an event-stream snapshot byte-identical to the pre-change baseline (no Plot-related events leak in).
src/plot-client/ unit tests prove the facade refuses to construct intent_edited / status_changed / attachment_removed / question_answered when the configured actor is agent:*.
scripts/acceptance/scenarios/25-plot-roundtrip.ts passes against a live warren+burrow stack and is idempotent on repeat runs (cleans up its Plot, run, and project).

warren/SPEC.md has a new Plot-integration subsection covering data flow, gating, ACL surface, and the env contract; warren/README.md and warren/CLAUDE.md list Plot as the fifth opt-in bundled feature; CHANGELOG.md has a v-next entry.
`bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` are all green with no warnings.

sections.context: Today the only way to drain an `sd plan` end-to-end is `scripts/run-plan.sh <plan-id>` on a workstation that must stay awake — every child seed is shelled into `claude -p "work on sd <id>" --permission-mode bypassPermissions` in a foreground loop. Warren already owns ephemeral cloud runs, branch push, PR open (`src/runs/reap.ts` lines 526–681 / mx-fd8619 / mx-b08612), and per-seed back-links (`runs.seed_id`, pl-bb70). It is the natural home for the walk-away half of plan execution. This seed adds a `PlanRun` primitive that takes one (plan_id, agent, project, model) tuple and walks the plan's open children sequentially — dispatching one warren run per child, waiting for that child's PR to merge before starting the next so each child's workspace branches off the prior child's merged commit. Parallel/DAG fan-out is explicitly out of scope (warren-fcc9 seed §'Explicitly out of scope'); resuming from a specific child stays manual (close completed children, re-dispatch). The integration is gated on `.seeds/` presence the same way `.plot/` is gated on `.plot/` (mx-96f004), so a project without seeds is unaffected. Once warren-fcc9 lands, warren-06dc (Phase 2) grows the `plot_id` field on PlanRun — that wiring is NOT in scope here.
sections.approach: Build PlanRun as an additive coordinator on top of the existing single-run primitive — never fork the spawn path. The coordinator is a second tick loop modeled on `src/triggers/tick.ts` (scheduler tick, single-flight via `bootScheduler`, pl-2f15 risk #5) that walks every active PlanRun once per `WARREN_PLAN_RUN_TICK_MS` (default 10s, lower than the 60s scheduler so the loop feels responsive). Each PlanRun has at most one in-flight child run; the tick's per-PlanRun decision is a small state machine: (a) no in-flight child + open pending children → dispatch the next; (b) in-flight child still running → noop; (c) in-flight child terminal-succeeded → poll the child's PR for `merged_at` via the existing GitHub REST seam (`src/runs/pr.ts` already authenticates with `GITHUB_TOKEN` and uses an injectable fetch per mx-b08612 / mx-b08612); merged → advance; not merged → noop; (d) in-flight child terminal-failed/cancelled → mark the PlanRun failed and stop; (e) the trivial-merge case where reap reported `commitsAhead=0` (mx-fd8619 — no PR was opened) → treat as 'merged trivially' and advance, so plans whose steps don't produce code (docs-only, sync-only) don't deadlock. Dispatch reuses `spawnRun` exactly (`src/runs/spawn.ts`), passing `seedId=<child seed>`, `trigger='plan-run'`, and a `metadata: { planRunId, planId, childSeq }` envelope so existing `runs` rows carry the back-link without schema gymnastics. The plan's open children are enumerated once at PlanRun creation via `sd plan show <id> --json` (`plan.children` is already a string array — verified against pl-2047) and persisted as `plan_run_children` rows; the tick re-reads each child's `sd show <id> --json` status to skip already-closed seeds on resume (warren-fcc9 acceptance: 'Re-dispatching the same plan id resumes from the next open child'). The 7-repo dialect-polymorphic adapter (mx-1d9f7a) is the schema substrate; a new `PlanRunsRepo` runs on `DrizzleAdapter` like every other repo (`src/db/repos/`). Storage of the prompt template lives on the PlanRun row, not derived: `promptTemplate` is a free-text string with one mustache-style placeholder `{seed_id}` substituted at dispatch; default is `'work on sd {seed_id}'` to match the run-plan.sh shape so operators get the legacy behavior with no input. UI ships two new pages (`NewPlanRun.tsx`, `PlanRunDetail.tsx`) plus a list page; navigation reuses the existing `NavBar` slot — the plot-shift work (warren-000b Phase 3) will eventually re-home this, but Phase 3 is out of scope. Gating on `.seeds/` introduces a `hasSeeds` boolean on `projects` mirroring `hasPlot` (warren-4e20) — the existing `PROJECT_FEATURE_DIRS` map in `src/projects/refresh.ts` already leaves room for it ('the seed plan ... leaves room for the existing opt-in integrations (`.mulch/`, `.seeds/`, `.canopy/`, `.pi/`) to start surfacing the same way'). The Plot integration deferred to warren-06dc is keptpurely additive — no `plot_id` column on `plan_runs`, no Plot writes from the coordinator. The serial-only contract is a feature, not a limitation (seed §Scope): subsequent children build on the prior PR's merged commit because spawnRun's `refreshProjectClone` (mx-1bb6 / `src/projects/refresh.ts`) fast-forwards the warren-side project clone before each dispatch, so the next burrow workspace inherits the latest main.

alternatives.name: Embed children as a JSON array on the plan_runs row instead of a separate plan_run_children table
alternatives.rejected_because: Children carry their own state machine (pending → dispatched → terminal → merged | failed), individual timestamps, individual run-id back-links, and per-child failure reasons. A JSON column would force every state advance to read-modify-write the whole array under a transaction — exactly the shape the `triggers` table already paid the cost to avoid (composite PK '<projectId>:<triggerId>' with direct upsert, mx-55296f). A separate child table with composite PK (planRunId, seq) is the same shape and queryable by index for the tick's hot path.

alternatives.name: Drive the coordinator off run-terminal events (event-driven push) instead of a polling tick
alternatives.rejected_because: The bridge's terminal-event observation (`src/runs/stream.ts` detectRuntimeTerminal, mx-a91bb9) fires before reap finishes the PR open. The coordinator needs reap's `prUrl` to poll GitHub for `merged_at`, so the event-driven version still ends up doing one round of polling. Single-flight tick is cheaper, simpler, restart-safe (no in-memory waiters to lose), and mirrors the established scheduler pattern (`src/triggers/tick.ts`).

alternatives.name: Re-derive children from `sd plan show` on every tick instead of persisting plan_run_children rows
alternatives.rejected_because: Re-shelling out to `sd` per tick per active PlanRun multiplies seeds-CLI calls (`SeedsCliDeps`, mx-fb4a85) by 6×/min/PlanRun. The plan's child list is immutable post-submit (seeds-side guarantee), so persisting once at PlanRun creation and re-reading the seed-status only when a child terminates is one read at create + N reads over the PlanRun's life, not N×ticks.

alternatives.name: Fan out parallel children whose `blockedBy` allows it (DAG-aware scheduling)
alternatives.rejected_because: Explicitly out of scope per the seed: 'Parallel / DAG-aware fan-out … pulls in stacked-PR tooling.' Parallel siblings on a shared base branch produce merge messes; sequential-with-merge-between is the V1 contract. File a follow-up after V1 lands if there is real demand.

alternatives.name: Block plan-run dispatch until `sd ready --plan <id>` lands upstream
alternatives.rejected_because: There is no `--plan` filter in seeds today (verified: `sd ready --help` shows only `--type/--label/--priority/...`). Replicating the 'next open child in plan order' selection locally is one `sd plan show <id> --json` (returns `plan.children` string array) plus per-child `sd show <id> --json` calls — both already shaped by the seeds-cli facade (`src/seeds-cli/extensions.ts`). Upstreaming `--plan` to seeds is a clean follow-up the coordinator can switch to without an API change.

alternatives.name: Wait on `git ls-remote origin <main>` to detect the prior PR's merge instead of polling the GitHub PR API
alternatives.rejected_because: An operator may rebase-and-merge, squash-merge, or merge a separate PR onto main between PlanRun children; remote HEAD movement is not a reliable proxy for 'this specific PR landed'. The PR's `merged_at` field is the authoritative source. `src/runs/pr.ts` already exposes the GitHub REST seam with the `GITHUB_TOKEN` auth path and injected fetch (mx-b08612), so this is one new `GET /pulls/:num` helper, not new infrastructure.

alternatives.name: Auto-cancel the in-flight child and roll back when a step fails so the operator can re-dispatch from scratch
alternatives.rejected_because: Reap is the only path that finalizes a non-terminal warren run (mx-fadaa2). Half-merged plans where step N committed and step N+1 failed are the normal failure mode for run-plan.sh today, and operators recover by inspecting and re-dispatching after fixing the failed seed (warren-fcc9: 'Re-dispatching the same plan id resumes from the next open child'). Auto-revert adds risk (clobbering legitimate work) for a workflow that's already manual.

steps.title: Project feature flag (.seeds/): widen src/projects/refresh.ts PROJECT_FEATURE_DIRS to add `seeds: '.seeds'` and ProjectFeatureFlags to add `hasSeeds: boolean`, add a `has_seeds` BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false column to `projects` in BOTH dialect schemas (src/db/schema/sqlite.ts AND src/db/schema/postgres.ts) — generate a new sqlite migration (`src/db/migrations/0014_*.sql`) and postgres migration (`src/db/migrations/postgres/0007_*.sql`) using `drizzle-kit generate` against drizzle.config.sqlite.ts / drizzle.config.postgres.ts respectively (mx-ddc0ec); the drift test src/db/schema/drift.test.ts (mx-de2250) must pass; extend ProjectsRepo's add/upsert to thread hasSeeds (mirror the hasPlot wiring landed in warren-4e20 / mx-96f004); extend `addProject` and `refreshProjectClone` callers in src/projects/manage.ts to persist the new field; extend the `ProjectRow` server-side type in src/ui/src/api/types.ts AND the GET /projects payload; extend src/projects/refresh.test.ts + src/projects/manage.test.ts with two new cases per the warren-4e20 test shape ('persists hasSeeds=true after probe', 'flips hasSeeds back to false when .seeds/ removed'); run `bun test src/projects src/db && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` before closing. This step is the smallest foundation and blocks the API surface (step 6) which validates `project.hasSeeds`.

DB schema + state machine: add `plan_runs` table to BOTH dialect schemas with columns (id TEXT PK, plan_id TEXT NOT NULL, project_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, agent_name TEXT NOT NULL, prompt_template TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'work on sd {seed_id}', ref TEXT, provider_override TEXT, model_override TEXT, dispatcher_handle TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'operator', trigger TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'manual', state TEXT NOT NULL CHECK enforced via TS-only narrowing per mx-2ab984, failure_reason TEXT, created_at TEXT NOT NULL, started_at TEXT, ended_at TEXT) with indexes (plan_runs_project_state ON (project_id, state), plan_runs_state ON state); add `plan_run_children` table (plan_run_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES plan_runs(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, seq INTEGER NOT NULL, seed_id TEXT NOT NULL, run_id TEXT REFERENCES runs(id) ON DELETE SET NULL, state TEXT NOT NULL ('pending'|'dispatched'|'running'|'pr_open'|'merged'|'failed'|'skipped'), created_at TEXT NOT NULL, updated_at TEXT NOT NULL, started_at TEXT, ended_at TEXT, pr_merged_at TEXT, failure_reason TEXT) with composite PK (plan_run_id, seq) and indexes (plan_run_children_run ON run_id, plan_run_children_state ON (plan_run_id, state)); add the new state-enum tuples PLAN_RUN_STATES = ['queued','running','succeeded','failed','cancelled'] and PLAN_RUN_CHILD_STATES = ['pending','dispatched','running','pr_open','merged','failed','skipped'] to src/db/schema/columns.ts; add the table + index names to TABLE_NAMES / INDEX_NAMES (mx-de2250 — drift test imports from here); regenerate migrations via `bun run --silent drizzle-kit generate --config drizzle.config.sqlite.ts` and the postgres counterpart, then hand-rename to descriptive `0015_add_plan_runs.sql` / `0008_add_plan_runs.sql` per the rename convention (mx-b4f9b5); migrations.test.ts must roundtrip; the drift test (src/db/schema/drift.test.ts) must pass without further hand-editing; build new repo `src/db/repos/plan-runs.ts` (PlanRunsRepo) on DrizzleAdapter following the dialect-polymorphic pattern (mx-1d9f7a) with methods: create({planRunRow, children}) (single tx via the adapter's tx helper that lands the parent row + N child rows atomically; mx-d5cf19 sqlite-async-tx pattern is the substrate), getById(id), listByProjectAndState, listActive() returning ('queued','running'), transitionTo(id, state, opts: {failureReason?, startedAt?, endedAt?}) with the state-machine guarded the same way RunsRepo.transitionTo guards runs (mx-a5432a), updateChild({planRunId, seq, patch}), and pickNextPending(planRunId) returning the lowest-seq pending child or null; mirror the partial-input pattern from RunsRepo.attachBurrow (mx-0a7a65) for child patches; extend src/db/repos/index.ts createRepos to construct + expose `planRuns`; ship src/db/repos/plan-runs.test.ts with: state-machine guards (no invalid transition), insertChildren idempotent on identical seq, pickNextPending returns null when all children !=pending, listActive omits terminal rows, and the tx rolls back the children when the parent insert violates a constraint (drop a duplicate id mid-tx). This is the substrate for steps 3, 5, 6.

Seeds CLI facade — plan + seed readers: extend src/seeds-cli/ with two new operations (a) `showPlan(deps, projectPath, planId): Promise<PlanShowResult>` shelling `sd plan show <id> --json` and parsing via a new Zod schema PlanShowEnvelopeSchema in src/seeds-cli/schema.ts (fields needed: success, plan.{id, status, children: string[], sections.steps: {title, blocks: number[]}[]}, .passthrough() per the convention that seeds may grow new fields, mx-... at src/seeds-cli/schema.ts); (b) `showSeed(deps, projectPath, seedId): Promise<SeedShowResult>` shelling `sd show <id> --json` and parsing for {id, status: 'open'|'closed', blockedBy: string[], extensions?: passthrough}; both go through the existing SeedsCliDeps + SpawnFn injection (mx-371491, src/seeds-cli/extensions.ts) so test stubs reuse the same shape; throw SeedsCliError with a copy-paste recoveryHint when the shell exits non-zero (mirror listScheduledSeeds error shape lines 50–60 of extensions.ts); export both + their result types through src/seeds-cli/index.ts so callers don't reach into the file layout; add src/seeds-cli/show.test.ts covering happy path, non-JSON output, envelope-mismatch, sd-exit-nonzero, and a parse-permissive 'plan has a new field we don't read' case (.passthrough must allow it through without failing); resist the urge to invent a `--plan` filter on sd ready — that's a clean follow-up to land in the seeds repo and is not blocking. This step blocks the coordinator (step 5) and the API handler (step 6) which use these readers.

PR-merge polling helper: extend src/runs/pr.ts with `checkPullRequestMerged({owner, repo, number, fetch, token}): Promise<CheckPrMergedResult>` where the result is a discriminated union {kind:'merged', mergedAt: string} | {kind:'open'} | {kind:'closed_unmerged'} | {kind:'missing_token'} | {kind:'http_error', status, message}; call GitHub REST `GET /repos/:owner/:repo/pulls/:number` with `Accept: application/vnd.github+json` and `Authorization: Bearer <token>` matching the existing posture in src/runs/pr.ts (line 13 'hits the GitHub REST API directly', auth via GITHUB_TOKEN per line 70); a new helper `parsePullRequestUrl(prUrl: string): {owner, repo, number} | null` regex-parses `https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/pull/<num>` returns null on mismatch (covers GHE-hosted shapes returning a null so the coordinator treats them as 'cannot verify merge' rather than 'merged'); add src/runs/pr.test.ts cases: missing token → kind:'missing_token' with the same skip-cleanly message style as opens (line 70); 200 with merged_at non-null → kind:'merged'; 200 with merged_at null + state:'open' → kind:'open'; 200 with state:'closed' + merged_at null → kind:'closed_unmerged'; 404 → kind:'http_error'; injected fetch matches the BurrowClient seam pattern (mx-b08612). Keep the helper a pure function — caller (coordinator step 5) decides what 'open' vs 'closed_unmerged' means for plan progression (open=wait, closed_unmerged=fail the plan with reason 'pr_closed_without_merge'). Step 6 (server API) does not need this; only the coordinator does.

Coordinator: build src/plan-runs/ module modeled on src/triggers/ (mx-fb4a85 single-flight tick is the load-bearing pattern). Files: index.ts (public exports), errors.ts (PlanRunDispatchError), tick.ts (top-level walker), coordinator.ts (per-PlanRun decision), dispatch.ts (spawn wrapper), pr-merge.ts (thin retry wrapper over the helper from step 4 that survives transient http_error). The per-PlanRun decision in coordinator.ts is `advancePlanRun({planRun, repos, listChildren, showPlan, showSeed, checkPrMerged, spawn, now, emit}): Promise<AdvanceResult>` returning a discriminated union {kind:'dispatched', childRunId} | {kind:'waiting_for_run'} | {kind:'waiting_for_merge'} | {kind:'advanced', mergedChildSeq, dispatchedChildSeq?} | {kind:'plan_failed', failedSeq, reason} | {kind:'plan_succeeded'} | {kind:'noop', reason}; state machine: (a) if planRun.state == 'queued' → transition to 'running', set startedAt, then fall through; (b) load the lowest-seq child whose state is in ('dispatched','running','pr_open'); if present and the linked run is non-terminal → waiting_for_run; if terminal-succeeded and child.state != 'pr_open' → flip child to 'pr_open' (or 'merged' if run.prUrl is null AND reap's empty-push event fired, treated as 'trivially merged' per mx-fd8619); if 'pr_open' → call checkPrMerged(run.prUrl); merged → flip child to 'merged' with mergedAt, fall through to dispatch next; open → waiting_for_merge; closed_unmerged or http 4xx → plan_failed with reason 'pr_closed_without_merge'; if terminal-failed/cancelled → plan_failed with reason `child_${reason ?? 'failed'}`; (c) if no in-flight child, call repos.planRuns.pickNextPending; if null → plan_succeeded (transition planRuns row to 'succeeded', set endedAt); if non-null → call showSeed against the child's seedId; status='closed' → flip to 'skipped' (resume semantics, warren-fcc9 acceptance), loop to (b); status='open' → spawn via dispatch.ts which calls spawnRun with {agentName: planRun.agentName, projectId: planRun.projectId, prompt: substitute(planRun.promptTemplate, {seed_id: child.seedId}), trigger: 'plan-run', seedId: child.seedId, providerOverride, modelOverride, ref: planRun.ref ?? project.defaultBranch, metadata: {planRunId, planId, childSeq}}; on success write child.runId, child.state='dispatched', child.startedAt; on RunSpawnError → plan_failed with reason `dispatch_failed:${err.message}`. tick.ts exports `runPlanRunTick(deps): Promise<PlanRunTickResult>` that lists all active planRuns via repos.planRuns.listActive(), advances each (catch-per-PlanRun like the scheduler's runProjectTick, mx-fb4a85 risk #9, no one bad row tears down the loop), and returns the result array for diagnostics. `bootPlanRunCoordinator(deps): {stop}` returns the same single-flight wrapper shape as `bootScheduler` (src/server/scheduler.ts) — drops overlapping ticks instead of stacking; honors `WARREN_PLAN_RUN_DISABLED=1` like `WARREN_SCHEDULER_DISABLED`; default cadence is `WARREN_PLAN_RUN_TICK_MS=10000` (10s, faster than scheduler's 60s). Surface every state transition as a warren event on the most recently-dispatched child run via the existing emit/event seam (mirror the scheduler's `trigger.*` system events) — kinds: `plan_run.advanced`, `plan_run.dispatched`, `plan_run.waiting_for_merge`, `plan_run.merged`, `plan_run.failed`, `plan_run.succeeded`. Unit tests in src/plan-runs/coordinator.test.ts cover all eight state-transition branches with stub repos + stub spawn + stub checkPrMerged; tick.test.ts covers the per-PlanRun isolation (one PlanRun throwing doesn't stop another). NO direct integration with sd update --extensions on plan-run dispatch — that's the seed back-link (pl-bb70) and is already handled by spawnRun's existing updateExtensions call when seedId is set (mx-ed7fb5 / src/runs/spawn.ts lines 395–401). This step depends on the DB repo (step 2), the seeds-cli readers (step 3), and the PR-merge helper (step 4).

Server API surface: extend src/server/handlers.ts with five new handlers + register routes. POST /plan-runs body: {project: string, planId: string, agent: string, promptTemplate?: string, ref?: string, providerOverride?: string, modelOverride?: string, dispatcherHandle?: string}; handler order: (1) load project via repos.projects.get; 404 if missing; (2) if !project.hasSeeds → ValidationError code:'project_lacks_seeds' with recoveryHint 'add a .seeds/ directory to the project clone and refresh' (mirror plot's reject shape at warren-a8c3); (3) call showPlan(seedsCli, project.localPath, planId); plan.status must be in ('approved','active','done'); empty open-children list → ValidationError code:'plan_has_no_open_children'; (4) resolve agent via existing repos.agents lookup with the scoped fallback (mx-644fb5); (5) build the plan_runs row with state='queued' + plan_run_children rows enumerated from plan.children in order, all state='pending'; (6) persist via repos.planRuns.create; (7) return 201 with the full row + children. GET /plan-runs?project=&state= lists with the same options-bag shape as listRunsHandler (mx-f1a881); GET /plan-runs/:id returns the row with embedded children AND a `runs` array fanned out from runs.listByIds(child.runId for each non-null) so the UI's detail page renders in one round-trip; POST /plan-runs/:id/cancel sets state='cancelled' and, if there is an in-flight child, also issues cancelRun against that child (existing seam, src/runs/cancel.ts); GET /plan-runs/:id/events tails the union of every child run's events via the broker (use the existing broker.subscribe + the events repo's listByRunIds; this is read-only so don't open a writer). Add the routes to the ROUTES table in handlers.ts (lines 1370–1378) and wire planRuns into ServerDeps in src/server/types.ts. Boot wiring in src/server/main.ts: instantiate the coordinator (`bootPlanRunCoordinator`) alongside bootScheduler with the same shutdown semantics (await coordinator.stop() before bridges.shutdown). Tests: extend src/server/handlers.test.ts (or a new handlers.plan-runs.test.ts following the workers/burrows split convention) covering each handler's happy path + the `project_lacks_seeds` rejection + the `plan_has_no_open_children` rejection + a CANCEL during in-flight that calls cancelRun. Depends on step 1 (hasSeeds flag), step 2 (DB), step 3 (seeds-cli readers), step 5 (the coordinator's bootPlanRunCoordinator + listActive shape).

UI surface: three new pages plus navigation + API client. (a) Extend src/ui/src/api/client.ts with planRunsApi = {list({project?, state?}), get(id), create(input), cancel(id), events(id) → NDJSON stream} and the corresponding types in src/ui/src/api/types.ts (PlanRunRow, PlanRunChildRow, CreatePlanRunInput, PlanRunDetailResponse including the fanned-out runs[]); types mirror the server payload shape (mx-… UI typesfile is wire-coupled, types.ts header comment lines 1–4); add hasSeeds to ProjectRow. (b) New page src/ui/src/pages/NewPlanRun.tsx adapted from NewRun.tsx (mx-… NewRun reuses the existing project/agent/model defaults flow at lines 30–95 of NewRun.tsx) but with a `planId` text input and a `promptTemplate` textarea defaulting to 'work on sd {seed_id}'; the form is disabled with a banner 'Plan runs require .seeds/' when the selected project's hasSeeds is false; on submit POST /plan-runs then navigate to /plan-runs/<id>; surface the agent/model defaults the same way NewRun does (defaultRole + defaultModel from .warren/defaults.json). (c) New page src/ui/src/pages/PlanRuns.tsx list view (modeled on src/ui/src/pages/Runs.tsx) showing plan_run_id, plan_id, project, agent, state, started_at, child counts (pending/in-flight/merged/failed). (d) New page src/ui/src/pages/PlanRunDetail.tsx with: header (state badge, plan_id link to seeds, agent, model, project, dispatcher_handle, started_at, ended_at, duration), child table (seq, seed_id with sd-link, run_id with /runs/:id link, state badge, started_at, ended_at, pr_url, pr_merged_at, failure_reason), Cancel button (POSTs /plan-runs/:id/cancel) visible only when state in ('queued','running'), live polling every 5s via tanstack-query's refetchInterval (same shape as RunDetail.tsx). (e) Add a 'Plans' top-level navigation entry in the App layout (or wherever NavBar lives) routing to /plan-runs; add /plan-runs/new for the create form and /plan-runs/:id for detail. Tests via vitest only if existing UI tests cover sibling pages (they don't — keep this step UI-only); run `bun run build:ui` and `bun run lint && bun run typecheck` from the warren root. NO Plot wiring (warren-06dc owns Phase 2; do not surface plot_id on the form). Depends on step 6 (server API) and step 1 (hasSeeds).

Acceptance scenario 26 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/26-plan-run-roundtrip.ts): end-to-end against a real warren+burrow stack mirroring scenario 22 (seeds extensions roundtrip, scripts/acceptance/scenarios/22-seeds-extensions-roundtrip.ts is the closest twin — read it before starting). Steps: (1) ensureSampleProject + reset source state (idempotent per mx-895738); (2) bootstrap `.seeds/config.yaml` (matches scenario 22 bootstrap lines 95–105) and append three open seed rows ah-acc-26-a/b/c — make c a docs-only seed whose dispatch will produce no commits (drives the trivial-merge branch); commit + refresh; (3) commit a real `.seeds/plans.jsonl` row pl-acc-26 with children [ah-acc-26-a, ah-acc-26-b, ah-acc-26-c] and status='approved' (mirror the schema in seeds-cli/schema.ts via a seed write rather than shelling sd plan submit so the harness stays deterministic, scenario 22 takes the same posture for issues); commit + refresh; (4) POST /plan-runs with {project, planId:'pl-acc-26', agent:<acceptance stub agent>, promptTemplate:'closeseed {seed_id}'}; assert 201 + state='queued' + 3 child rows in 'pending'; (5) poll GET /plan-runs/:id every 1s up to a generous deadline (60s — sequential 3 children at acceptance speed); the acceptance stub agent (scripts/acceptance/lib/stub-agent/agent.sh, mx-8f313a) is parameterizable via prompt knobs — pass `closeseed <id>` so the agent invokes `sd close <id>` then exits 0; for the docs-only child, also pass `--no-commit` so reap reports commitsAhead=0 (the trivial-merge case must advance without GH polling); (6) for children that DO produce commits, stub the GH PR-merge call by injecting a `WARREN_GH_FETCH_OVERRIDE` env (introduce one if missing — small dial in src/runs/pr.ts and src/plan-runs/pr-merge.ts) so checkPullRequestMerged returns kind:'merged' immediately for the test pull URL; (7) assertions on the terminal state: plan_runs row state='succeeded'; all three plan_run_children state='merged' (last one via the trivial-merge path); runs.listByIds(child.runId) returns three rows each with trigger='plan-run' and metadata.planRunId set; events stream surfaces `plan_run.advanced` + `plan_run.merged` + `plan_run.succeeded`; (8) a SECOND POST /plan-runs against the SAME plan id after closing ah-acc-26-b out of band must return 201 then immediately advance ah-acc-26-b to 'skipped' state without dispatching a child run, asserting the resume semantics (warren-fcc9 acceptance: 're-dispatching the same plan id resumes from the next open child'); (9) idempotent cleanup of any source-repo seed rows (matches scenario 22 lines 600s teardown); scenarios.ts registry update (the auto-registration via numeric prefix means just creating the file is enough — verify by re-reading scripts/acceptance/index.ts or wherever the manifest lives). Modes: ['in-proc'] only (matches scenario 22 — drives source-repo edits the container harness doesn't bind-mount, mx-1d31f0 is the container-only counterexample). Depends on step 5 (coordinator) and step 6 (server API).

SPEC + docs + CHANGELOG: (a) add a new subsection §11.P 'PlanRun: serial plan execution' to warren/SPEC.md immediately after §11.O (Plot integration), covering: data model (plan_runs + plan_run_children, sequencing contract), gating (.seeds/ + hasSeeds), the coordinator state machine (the 6-kind AdvanceResult union from step 5), the trivial-merge case for zero-commit children, the PR-merge polling contract (GITHUB_TOKEN + merged_at), the resume semantics on re-dispatch, the env vars (WARREN_PLAN_RUN_TICK_MS, WARREN_PLAN_RUN_DISABLED), and the explicit Plot-disjoint scope note pointing at warren-06dc for Phase 2; (b) update warren/README.md feature list to mention PlanRun as a new built-in (NOT a fifth bundled feature — it's a coordinator on top of existing primitives, frame it as a dispatch mode); update the standalone-vs-integration framing per warren/CLAUDE.md line 'When you change cross-cutting docs (README, SPEC §1/§2, package description), keep the standalone path primary and the integrations as features that light up when used' — Plan-run lights up when .seeds/ is present; (c) update warren/CLAUDE.md's brief feature listing to add 'plan-run — serial sd plan execution; activated by the project having a .seeds/ directory'; (d) CHANGELOG.md entry under the next unreleased version following the existing changelog style (look at the v0.3.16 entry for shape); update src/index.ts VERSION constant + package.json version following the dual-bump convention (warren/CLAUDE.md 'Version Management' section); (e) record mulch insights via `ml learn` then `ml record` for any new conventions surfaced (the coordinator state machine, the trivial-merge advance rule, the resume-on-re-dispatch contract — each one a separate record so search-by-domain stays useful, mx-… planning domain); (f) run the full trinity `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` from the warren root AND the acceptance harness (`bun run acceptance --scenario 26` per scripts/acceptance/README if it exists, otherwise the runner the repo uses) before marking the plan done; (g) close warren-fcc9 via `sd close warren-fcc9` then `sd sync && ml sync && git push`. Depends on every prior step; this is the final integration + ship step.

risks: PR-merge polling rate-limits warren's GITHUB_TOKEN under many concurrent active PlanRuns. Mitigation: tick cadence 10s × at most one in-flight child per PlanRun × authenticated GitHub limit 5000/hr means an org running 50 concurrent PlanRuns burns ~1800 calls/hr, well under cap. Coordinator polls only when a child run is in 'pr_open' — not every tick. If the limit is ever hit, the helper's kind:'http_error' result + the per-PlanRun catch keeps the tick alive; operators raise the cadence env or wait it out.

Schema drift between sqlite and postgres dialects after hand-editing migrations. Mitigation: run src/db/schema/drift.test.ts after every schema change (mx-de2250) — it imports both dialect schemas via drizzle-kit's getTableConfig and asserts column-list + index-list parity. The cross-dialect row-passthrough pattern (mx-845f11) relies on this. Also rerun migrations.test.ts which boots both dialects against the generated SQL.

Coordinator races with bridge: the bridge mirrors queued→running but reap owns terminal transitions (mx-30a49a / mx-98a470). The coordinator must not read child.run.state directly without going through repos.runs.get which reflects only writer transitions. Mitigation: read run rows via repos.runs.get exclusively; never read live burrow state from the coordinator. The state machine in step 5 is keyed on the warren-side runs row only. The reap transition is the moment a child becomes eligible to advance (terminal state observable); the bridge's queued→running mirror is irrelevant to the coordinator.

Resume-on-re-dispatch double-spawns a child whose seed was closed by the previous attempt but whose plan_run_children row is stuck in 'dispatched' because warren crashed mid-flight. Mitigation: on PlanRun creation, the coordinator's first tick MUST call showSeed for every child whose state is 'pending' AND for every child currently in 'dispatched'/'running' state on the PREVIOUS PlanRun for the same planId+projectId, transitioning closed seeds to 'skipped' BEFORE attempting any new dispatch. Persist this 'crash recovery sweep' explicitly in step 2's PlanRunsRepo (a `reconcile(planRunId, seedStatuses)` method) and exercise it via scenario 26 step (8).

Sequential semantic breaks when an operator manually opens a stacked PR between PlanRun children, advancing main past the previous child's merge. Mitigation: acceptable — each spawn calls refreshProjectClone which fast-forwards to remote main before the child workspace is provisioned (mx-1bb6). The child branches off whatever main is at dispatch time, NOT off the previous child's merge commit specifically. Document this in SPEC §11.P explicitly so operators know stacked-merge interleavings during a PlanRun are tolerated, not corrupted.

showPlan output schema drifts as seeds adds new plan fields, breaking the parser. Mitigation: the Zod schema uses .passthrough() at every level (matches the existing SeedExtensionsSchema posture in src/seeds-cli/schema.ts) so unknown fields are tolerated. Only the fields warren reads are required. Lock the contract with a unit test that parses a real `sd plan show pl-2047 --json` capture committed under src/seeds-cli/__fixtures__/.

PostgreSQL CHECK constraint on plan_run_state would require a migration when we add a new state. Mitigation: don't use SQL CHECK constraints — TS-only narrowing via the enum tuple in src/db/schema/columns.ts (mx-2ab984). Drizzle's `text({enum})` is TS-only by design; new states ship as a one-line tuple change + a migration only for index additions, not state expansions.
acceptance: POST /plan-runs against a project with .seeds/ + a valid sd plan id returns 201 with state='queued' and one plan_run_children row per open child in plan-order; against a project without .seeds/ rejects 400 with code:'project_lacks_seeds'; against a non-existent plan or one with no open children rejects 400 with the typed error code.

An end-to-end run of acceptance scenario 26 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/26-plan-run-roundtrip.ts) succeeds: three children dispatch in order, each waiting for the prior's PR merge (or trivial-merge for zero-commit children), the PlanRun terminates with state='succeeded', and re-dispatching the same plan id after manually closing one child skips it via the 'skipped' state without re-running the agent.

A child run dispatched by the coordinator has trigger='plan-run', seedId=<the child's seed>, and metadata.planRunId pointing at the parent; existing seedId-driven extension wiring (pl-bb70) stamps {role, trigger:'plan-run', lastRunId, lastRunAt} on the seed via sd update --extensions — verified by scenario 26 reading the project's .seeds/issues.jsonl post-completion.

Single-run dispatch (POST /runs) is byte-identical to the warren-fcc9 baseline — diffing the pre/post create response payloads for an identical input produces no field changes; the existing /runs handler tests in src/server/handlers.test.ts pass without modification.

Boot the server with WARREN_PLAN_RUN_DISABLED=1 and POST /plan-runs still inserts the row (the API surface is independent of the coordinator); without the disable, the coordinator tick advances within `WARREN_PLAN_RUN_TICK_MS` of dispatch — verified via the coordinator unit tests in src/plan-runs/tick.test.ts.

POST /plan-runs/:id/cancel during an in-flight child transitions the PlanRun to 'cancelled' AND issues cancelRun against the in-flight child; an already-terminal PlanRun returns the unchanged state with `alreadyTerminal:true`.

src/db/schema/drift.test.ts and src/db/migrations/migrations.test.ts pass after the new schema lands; the postgres migration mirror exists and the rls migration pattern (0002_enable_rls.sql, mx-889760) is updated to grant RLS on the two new tables.

`bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` succeeds from the warren root with zero warnings (biome --error-on-warnings is the gate, warren/CLAUDE.md 'Quality Gates'); the new acceptance scenario 26 runs green in the in-proc mode.

Documentation lands: SPEC.md §11.P exists and references the coordinator state machine + env vars; README.md and CLAUDE.md mention plan-run as a `.seeds/`-gated dispatch mode; CHANGELOG.md has a new entry; package.json + src/index.ts VERSION are bumped consistently (warren/CLAUDE.md 'Version Management' both-files-or-CI-fails).

sections.context: warren-06dc is Phase 2 of the Plot adoption epic (warren-000b). Phase 1 (pl-2047, closed) wired Plot into single-run dispatch — `runs.plot_id` column, `PLOT_ID`/`PLOT_ACTOR` env injection at spawn (src/runs/spawn.ts:671), `run_dispatched` event emission via `defaultPlotAppender` (spawn.ts:590), and JSONL-tail-at-reap mirroring (src/runs/reap.ts:1259). PlanRun V1 (pl-a258, just landed) built a serial plan-run coordinator on top of the single-run primitive (src/plan-runs/coordinator.ts) but explicitly deferred Plot wiring (warren-a87f: 'NO Plot wiring; warren-06dc owns Phase 2'). This plan adds the same wiring onto PlanRun so a plan-run launched from a Plot threads context through every child and reports lifecycle back to the Plot. Composition is purely additive (per warren-fcc9 acceptance: 'existing single-run dispatch path is unchanged'). Because the coordinator already routes every child through `spawnRun` (src/plan-runs/dispatch.ts), the per-child `run_dispatched` and PLOT_ID/PLOT_ACTOR injection light up automatically once the coordinator forwards `plot_id` into the spawn input — most of the work is plumbing one nullable field through the DB, API, coordinator, and UI.
