The SQLite index at .plot/.index.db is derived state and is gitignored per ../plot/README.md; do not preserve it across the reset (let it rebuild via the existing rebuildIndex retry-once path, mx-239786). File a follow-up seed for shape (a) — commit `.plot/` through reap so it round-trips to origin and survives across machines — that's a separate, larger change. Alternative considered and rejected for now: (a) commit-through-reap delivers durability to origin but trivial-merge children with no agent commits would carry no Plot writes home; (c) out-of-tree side-table mirroring warren events is a new persistence surface that diverges Plot's on-disk shape from what `plot show` reads. Shape (b) is single-function, restores correctness today, and doesn't commit warren to a side-table.

steps.title: Snapshot+restore .plot/ in refreshProjectClone
steps.description: Modify src/projects/refresh.ts refreshProjectClone: before the `git reset --hard` block (line ~137), if `exists(path.join(localPath, '.plot'))`, snapshot the `.plot/` directory's tracked + untracked file contents (events.jsonl, json) to a temp dir under os.tmpdir() with a unique name. Skip `.plot/.index.db` (derived, gitignored). After the reset completes (and the user.name/user.email --unset-all loop, line ~155), restore every snapshotted file by writing it back to its original path under `.plot/`; create parent dirs as needed. On any error during snapshot or restore, surface a typed error (define `PlotPreservationError extends ProjectUnavailableError` in src/projects/errors.ts, or reuse ProjectUnavailableError with a clear message) — do NOT silently swallow, because data loss is the whole point of this fix. The snapshot temp dir is cleaned up in a finally block.

steps.title: Unit test: refresh preserves .plot/ appends
steps.description: Add tests to src/projects/refresh.test.ts (or create if it doesn't exist) using the existing fixture pattern. Three cases: (a) a project with `.plot/<id>.events.jsonl` containing 3 appended events survives a refreshProjectClone call — assert byte-equality after refresh; (b) a project with `.plot/<id>.json` containing `{status: 'done'}` (where origin's committed version has `status: 'active'`) survives — the local-side `done` wins on restore; (c) a project WITHOUT `.plot/` is byte-identical to today's refresh behavior — no extra fs calls, no error. Mock git via the existing spawn-fn injection; mock fs only where needed for the snapshot/restore paths. Idempotency: running refreshProjectClone twice in a row with no intervening writes is a no-op.

steps.title: Remove scenario 27's 100ms tail workaround
steps.description: In scripts/acceptance/scenarios/27-plan-run-plot-roundtrip.ts, locate the 100ms tail loop that accumulates transient `.plot/<id>.events.jsonl` writes into an in-memory log (search for `setInterval(100` or `tail` or comments referencing this workaround). Remove the loop. Re-source the assertions from the on-disk events.jsonl directly at the end of the plan-run. The plan_run_dispatched event, per-child run_dispatched events, and the final status_changed event should all be present in the durable on-disk file. Update inline comments to remove references to the workaround. Run scenario 27 against a live stack to confirm green.

steps.title: Follow-up seed: shape (a) commit-through-reap
steps.description: Create a new seed in warren's .seeds/ titled 'Commit .plot/ through reap so Plot history reaches origin' with type=task priority=medium. Description: shape (b) preserves .plot/ across refreshes in a single working tree, but doesn't round-trip Plot writes to origin — so a fresh clone on another machine has no history of warren's host-side appends. Shape (a) layers on top: at reap time (src/runs/reap.ts), after the agent's workspace branch is pushed, also stage and commit any .plot/<id>.{events.jsonl,json} changes warren made (filter to host-side-modified files via the snapshot diff or by tracking append targets in spawn.ts) on a separate commit (or amended into the same workspace push) so they ride along. Link to this seed and to the plot mulch records (mx-a224cf, mx-c4307b). Do NOT implement here — scope of warren-6f25 ends with shape (b).

steps.title: SPEC + CHANGELOG + warren patch bump + ml records
steps.description: Update warren/SPEC.md §11.O (Plot dispatch) and §11.P (PlanRun) to note: `.plot/` is preserved across refreshProjectClone via in-process snapshot+restore (shape b); origin-side durability is deferred to the follow-up seed (link). Add a CHANGELOG entry under a new patch-version section describing the fix and naming the scenario 27 tail-workaround removal. Hand-bump package.json `"version"` and src/index.ts VERSION consistently. Record ml insights: a `failure`-type record in the plots domain naming the original bug + the (b) resolution; a `convention` record on the snapshot-restore pattern in src/projects/refresh.ts so future agents reading refresh.ts know why `.plot/` is special-cased. Run quality-gate trinity (bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck) with zero warnings.

acceptance: After a plan-run with N>=2 children completes against a project with `.plot/`, the on-disk `.plot/<id>.events.jsonl` contains every plan_run_dispatched event, every per-child run_dispatched event, AND the auto-done status_changed event — none are lost to refreshProjectClone resets.

After autoTransitionPlotToDone fires, the on-disk `.plot/<id>.json` reports `status: "done"` and that value is durable across a subsequent refreshProjectClone call against the same project clone — the snapshot's `done` wins over origin's older `active`.

scripts/acceptance/scenarios/27-plan-run-plot-roundtrip.ts passes against a live warren+burrow stack WITHOUT the 100ms tail workaround — assertions read directly from the on-disk events.jsonl at the end.

src/projects/refresh.test.ts has new tests covering: (a) byte-equality preservation of events.jsonl across refresh, (b) local-`done` status.json wins over origin-`active` on restore, (c) projects without `.plot/` are byte-identical to today's refresh.
A project WITHOUT `.plot/` is byte-identical to today's refreshProjectClone behavior — no extra fs operations, no errors, no changes to its features detection result.
Snapshot temp dirs are cleaned up after every refresh call — no leaks under os.tmpdir() after 100 consecutive refreshes (smoke-test in the test file).
A follow-up seed for shape (a) commit-through-reap is filed in warren's .seeds/ with a clear description and links back to this seed.
SPEC.md §11.O / §11.P document the preservation contract and the deferred origin-durability work.
CHANGELOG.md has a new patch-version entry; package.json `"version"` and src/index.ts VERSION are bumped consistently.
ml records exist for the failure (plots domain) and the snapshot-restore convention (build or projects domain).
`bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` succeeds with zero warnings (biome --error-on-warnings gate).

sections.context: Plot was adopted as warren's coordination primitive (mx-39fd5c) to make humans and agents peer nodes on a shared per-Plot event log. Dogfooding plot-3e72876d (the 9-seed housekeeping pass that motivated this epic) surfaced the gap: a user with a Plot bound to N seeds_issue attachments still has to (a) navigate to /runs/new, (b) manually select project + agent + seed prompt, and (c) type the plot id by hand — for every child seed. User-reported friction quote: 'right now I can see the plot which is great, but now I need to manually go and trigger all these runs which isn't ideal.' The epic closes that loop: PlotDetail becomes the launchpad. Plots become plans become seeds become runs, with the UI making that flow as easy as possible. Three already-filed child seeds (warren-ff2a closed/shipped, warren-ea66, warren-5561) plus a validation-hardening bug (warren-a353) discovered mid-epic when a user pasted 'plot_id=plot-3e72876d' verbatim into the NewRun plot_id input and warren silently accepted it. This plan adopts all four into a single sequenced shipment. Related: warren-bb22 (Plot discoverability — dogfood signal #1), warren-ede7 (aggregator empty-rows — signal #2, shipped 0.4.4). Out of scope: anything that isn't surfacing Plot-bound dispatch in the UI.
sections.approach: Ship in dependency order, smallest-correct-intervention first. Step 1 (already shipped as warren-ff2a) wires the per-seed Run button — the foundational single-dispatch primitive that pre-fills project/agent/plot_id/prompt into NewRun. Step 2 hardens the dispatch surface (warren-a353): client + server validation of plot_id format before any new caller (steps 3 and 4) can amplify the silent-failure footprint by dispatching N runs against a malformed Plot id. Step 3 (warren-ea66) layers a batch Dispatch all attached seeds button on top of step 1's primitive — N parallel runs, or N serial runs as a stop-gap until step 4 lands. Step 4 (warren-5561) is the deepest realization: Plot→synthesized seeds plan→plan-run pipeline, which requires a cross-repo seeds change (sd plan submit accepting existing-seed children) and a new warren endpoint that synthesizes the plan + dispatches plan-run with plot_id pre-filled, reusing every pl-7937 wiring (plan_run_dispatched event, per-child PLOT_ID injection, auto-done on final merge). Each step is independently shippable and adds user-visible value on its own. Note on plan mechanics: because sd plan submit cannot currently adopt existing seeds (that capability IS step 4), this plan's steps mirror the four existing seed descriptions verbatim; after submission we reconcile spawned-vs-original (close the throwaway side, keep the canonical IDs that mulch records already reference).

alternatives.name: Webhook-driven auto-dispatch on Plot status transitions
alternatives.rejected_because: e.g. drafting→ready fires runs against all open seeds_issue attachments. Too magic for V1, removes the human confirm step, and depends on GitHub App auth infra (R-18) which isn't shipped. Keep dispatch human-initiated; revisit after R-18 lands.

alternatives.name: Build the plan-run pipeline (step 4) first without the batch button (step 3)
alternatives.rejected_because: Step 4 requires a cross-repo seeds release and double-pin (per the burrow precedent); step 3 is a pure warren-side change that ships value in days. Ordering = dependency-driven + size-ordered. Step 3 also acts as a stop-gap for serial dispatch while step 4 is in flight.

alternatives.name: Skip plot_id validation (warren-a353) until a user reports a real broken run
alternatives.rejected_because: Steps 3 and 4 dispatch N runs against the same plot_id, so a single malformed input would silently break N appender calls. Amplification risk makes hardening a prereq, not a follow-up. The motivating bug (literal 'plot_id=plot-3e72876d' string accepted) already happened once at N=1.

alternatives.name: Make the Run button a one-click no-confirm dispatch
alternatives.rejected_because: The user must be able to edit the prompt before submission (and review project/agent/plot_id). Pre-fill-then-edit is the chosen shape; eliminating the edit step would force the user back into /runs/new to tweak anything, defeating the friction-reduction goal.

steps.title: Step 1 (warren-ff2a fold-in, SHIPPED): per-seeds_issue-attachment Run agent button on PlotDetail
steps.description: ALREADY SHIPPED as warren-ff2a — included here for plan completeness. On src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx, in the SubstratePanel where seeds_issue attachments are rendered, add a small Run agent button next to each attachment. Click opens NewRun.tsx pre-filled with: project (from the Plot's owning project, resolved via PlotResolver), agent (the project default per .warren/defaults.json defaultRole), plot_id (this Plot's id), and prompt seeded from the seed's title + description via a project-default plot-run prompt template (or the .warren/defaults.json defaultPrompt with seed_id substitution). The user can still edit any field before submitting. Implementation: query params or route state into NewRun, NewRun reads on mount and seeds initial form state. Acceptance: (a) every seeds_issue attachment on PlotDetail renders a Run agent button, (b) clicking opens NewRun.tsx with project/agent/plot_id/prompt pre-filled, (c) the dispatched run lands in the Plot's activity feed within one poll tick, (d) detaching the attachment removes the button without re-render flicker, (e) no behavior change for non-seeds_issue attachments. Status: closed via commit a08525f / warren-ff2a — this plan step should be closed immediately on submission and reference warren-ff2a as the canonical record.

steps.title: Step 2 (warren-a353 fold-in): validate plot_id format and reject malformed at dispatch
steps.description: Dogfood signal #4 from plot-3e72876d: a run was dispatched via the new NewRun.tsx plot_id input with the input field containing the literal string 'plot_id=plot-3e72876d' (user pasted the prose form including the key= prefix). The server accepted it; the host-side defaultPlotAppender (src/runs/spawn.ts) tried to append on a Plot resolved by that malformed id; the lookup quietly failed (caught and logged best-effort); the only user-visible signal is the run detail page's PlotMetaCardContent rendering 'Plot no longer available' long after dispatch. This step hardens the surface before steps 3 and 4 amplify the failure mode by dispatching N runs at once. FIX SHAPE: (A) CLIENT — in src/ui/src/pages/NewRun.tsx and src/ui/src/pages/NewPlanRun.tsx, validate the trimmed plot_id against /^plot-[a-z0-9]+$/ on submit; if non-empty and non-matching, show an inline error 'Plot ID must look like plot-xxxxxxxx' and block the Dispatch button. Apply to BOTH forms. (B) SERVER — in src/server/handlers.ts on POST /runs (and POST /plan-runs), when plot_id is present, validate format via the same regex AND resolve the Plot via PlotResolver BEFORE the run row is inserted; reject with a typed error (extend src/runs/errors.ts — code='plot_id_not_found' or 'plot_id_invalid', recoveryHint='Verify the Plot exists at /plots') and return 400. (C) Acceptance: (1) submitting plot_id='plot_id=plot-3e72876d' shows the inline error and blocks Dispatch on client; (2) curl with the same malformed string gets 400 with code='plot_id_invalid'; (3) submitting well-formed but non-existent plot_id gets 400 with code='plot_id_not_found'; (4) well-formed and existing succeeds as today; (5) submitting no plot_id at all is byte-identical to current behavior; (6) the existing best-effort appender logging path is unchanged (only fires on success now); (7) unit tests pin all five cases; (8) bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck green with zero warnings. Single warren PR, no cross-repo. Adopt warren-a353 as the canonical seed.

steps.title: Step 3 (warren-ea66 fold-in): PlotDetail batch Dispatch all attached seeds button
steps.description: On src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx, add a Dispatch all attached seeds button to the page header (or a dedicated 'Coordinate' section). Behavior: enumerates all seeds_issue attachments whose underlying seeds are still open (closed seeds skipped, matching plan-run resume semantics from pl-a258), then fires POST /runs for each one in parallel (default) or serial-gated-on-PR-merge (opt-in toggle). All dispatched runs are bound to this plot_id via the same path step 1 uses. Toggle UI: a checkbox 'Serial (gate next on prior PR merge)' next to the button, off by default. When serial mode is selected and step 4 has shipped, warren creates ONE plan-run wrapping the open attachments. Stop-gap (before step 4 lands): warren queues runs internally and dispatches them in sequence as each prior PR merges. Confirm-dialog before firing N runs: 'Dispatch N runs against this Plot? Estimated cost: <X> (sum of per-run estimates).' Acceptance: (a) button visible whenever Plot has >=1 open seeds_issue attachment, (b) clicking (parallel mode) creates N runs all bound to plot_id, all visible in /runs and in this Plot's activity feed within one poll tick, (c) clicking (serial mode) creates 1 plan-run with N child runs (post step 4) or the stop-gap serial queue (pre step 4), (d) closed-seed attachments are skipped without spawning, (e) confirm-dialog blocks accidental fire. All NewRun-style dispatches inherit the step 2 plot_id validation by construction. Adopt warren-ea66 as the canonical seed.

steps.title: Step 4 (warren-5561 fold-in): Plot→synthesized seeds plan→plan-run pipeline (cross-repo)
steps.description: The deepest 'Plots become plans become seeds become runs' realization. Today sd plan submit spawns NEW children; you can't make existing seeds children of a new plan. That blocks the cleanest Plot-as-orchestrator shape: a Plot's seeds_issue attachments synthesize into a seeds plan whose children ARE those attached seeds, then plan-run drives them with all existing serial-gating, PR-merge tracking, and auto-done-on-final-merge semantics for free. Two-part work: (A) cross-repo seeds change — extend sd plan submit (or add sd plan from-existing) to accept an existing_seeds: [warren-X, warren-Y, ...] field. Plan children become references to those seeds, not new spawns. Validation: every existing seed must already exist in .seeds/issues.jsonl; closed seeds rejected at plan-creation time (plan-run already skips closed children at execution, but creation should be intentional). (B) warren-side — PlotDetail surfaces a 'Dispatch as plan-run' button (in addition to step 3's batch button). Click: warren server synthesizes a plan via the new seeds API, calls POST /plan-runs against the synthesized plan with plot_id pre-filled, navigates to /plan-runs/:id. Reuses every existing pl-7937 (warren-06dc) wiring — plan_run_dispatched event on the Plot, per-child PLOT_ID/PLOT_ACTOR injection, auto-done transition when the final child merges. Acceptance: (a) cross-repo: seeds ships sd plan submit support for existing-seed children with a unit test; (b) cross-repo: warren consumes the new seeds version (double-pin per the burrow precedent) and exposes the pipeline via a new endpoint POST /plot-plan-runs (or similar); (c) PlotDetail's 'Dispatch as plan-run' button fires the pipeline end-to-end against plot-3e72876d-shaped Plots (10 seeds_issue attachments → 1 plan-run with 10 children → auto-done on final merge); (d) existing pl-7937 / pl-a258 flows unchanged (no regression in current plan-run dispatch). Largest of the four steps; ships last because cross-repo. Bonus: shipping this also retroactively unblocks the 'adopt existing seeds into a plan' mechanic that this very plan had to work around. Adopt warren-5561 as the canonical seed.

risks: sd plan submit cannot adopt existing seeds (step 4 IS this fix), so this plan must spawn duplicates and reconcile post-submission. Mitigation: keep originals (warren-ff2a, warren-a353, warren-ea66, warren-5561) as canonical and close the throwaway spawns immediately, pointing them at the originals — mulch records and a353's description already reference the originals by id.

Step 2 (plot_id validation) tightens behavior that some in-flight runs may rely on (well-formed-but-non-existent plot_ids that were previously accepted and silently no-opped). Mitigation: grep deployed warren logs for malformed plot_ids before rolling out; the only known offender is the one that motivated a353.

Step 4's cross-repo release requires seeds CLI + warren double-pin (Dockerfile + package.json + bun.lock, per the burrow precedent). Mitigation: follow the documented cross-repo-design-lock-seed pattern (mx-bffcf1) — freeze the seeds API in SPEC first, file the seeds dependency seed, then ship in lockstep.

Step 3's stop-gap serial-queue mode adds a code path that step 4 then deprecates. Mitigation: gate the stop-gap behind 'step 4 not yet shipped' rather than build it permanently; remove on step 4 land.

Confirm-dialog cost estimate in step 3 needs per-run estimates from somewhere (warren-config defaults? per-agent fixed estimate?). Mitigation: scope to a coarse 'N runs × default-per-run-budget' display for V1; tighten later.
acceptance: Step 1 closed as already-shipped via warren-ff2a; spawned duplicate closed and linked to ff2a.
Step 2 (warren-a353): client + server reject malformed plot_id with typed errors; all 8 sub-acceptance criteria green; bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck pass with zero warnings.

Step 3 (warren-ea66): batch Dispatch button on PlotDetail dispatches N runs in parallel or serial-stop-gap; all dispatched runs land in the Plot's activity feed within one poll tick; closed-seed attachments skipped; confirm-dialog gates accidental fire.

Step 4 (warren-5561): seeds ships existing-seeds plan support; warren double-pins and exposes /plot-plan-runs (or named endpoint); PlotDetail 'Dispatch as plan-run' button runs the full pipeline against a plot-3e72876d-shaped Plot (10 attachments → 1 plan-run with 10 children → auto-done on final merge); no regression in pl-7937 / pl-a258 flows.

Dogfood validation: a fresh housekeeping-style Plot (≥5 seeds_issue attachments) can be dispatched end-to-end from PlotDetail in three clicks or fewer (button → confirm → done), with zero manual /runs/new navigation and zero hand-typed plot_ids.
Epic-level: warren-e40a closed when steps 2-4 are merged; warren-bb22 (sibling, Plot discoverability) remains tracked separately.

sections.context: pl-5310 step 4 (warren-a4b7) is the deepest realization of the Plot UX vision (warren-e40a): a Plot becomes a plan becomes seeds become runs, with the seeds plan synthesized on the fly from the Plot's seeds_issue attachments. Steps 1-3 of pl-5310 shipped the per-seed Run button (warren-ff2a), plot_id validation (warren-a353/warren-bae5), and the batch parallel/serial dispatcher (warren-7c3f). What is still missing is the synthesis path: today neither the per-seed button nor the batch dispatcher produces a tracked PlanRun row, neither inherits pl-7937's auto-done-on-final-merge hook, neither reuses the existing /plan-runs/:id detail page as the launch-and-watch surface. SPEC §11.Q (landed in this commit) design-locks the shape. This sub-plan walks the implementation in dependency order, starting with the cross-repo upstream blocker.
sections.approach: Mirror the cross-repo-design-lock-seed pattern (mx-bffcf1, used by warren-94d8 for R-19 / §11.L). Phase 1 (this commit) ships the SPEC §11.Q design lock + the upstream seeds-cli tracker seed (warren-d519). Phase 2 ships seeds-cli's existing-seed children support (out-of-repo work, tracked by warren-d519). Phase 3 lockstep-bumps warren's seeds-cli pin (package.json + bun.lock + Dockerfile per the burrow-cli rule). Phase 4 ships the warren-side synthesis module (src/plot-plan-runs/) and POST /plot-plan-runs endpoint, reusing every §11.P.Plot wiring unchanged. Phase 5 wires the PlotDetail 'Dispatch as plan-run' button on top. Phase 6 lands acceptance scenario 30 composing scenarios 25 + 27 + 29 against a real warren+burrow stack. Each warren-side step is independently shippable on top of seeds-cli's released capability; before seeds-cli ships, only the design lock + tracker land.

alternatives.name: Skip the synthesis endpoint, surface a 'Copy plan JSON' button instead and have users run sd plan submit themselves
alternatives.rejected_because: Reintroduces manual /runs/new-style friction the epic exists to eliminate. The user-reported friction quote ('right now I can see the plot which is great, but now I need to manually go and trigger all these runs which isn't ideal') applies just as much to manually invoking sd plan submit. Dispatch must be one click from PlotDetail.

alternatives.name: Build synthesis client-side in the browser instead of server-side
alternatives.rejected_because: Synthesis requires shelling out to sd plan submit, which only the warren host has. Doing it server-side also keeps the seeds-cli double-pin in one place and matches the existing pattern for shelling out to sd from warren handlers.

alternatives.name: Reuse POST /plan-runs with an inline-synthesis discriminator instead of POST /plot-plan-runs
alternatives.rejected_because: Mixing synthesis-and-dispatch into the explicit-plan endpoint blurs two distinct contracts. /plot-plan-runs makes the synthesis step explicit at the URL, keeps /plan-runs untouched (zero regression risk for pl-7937 / pl-a258 flows), and gives the UI a clean route for the confirm-dialog branching.

steps.title: seeds-cli: ship 'sd plan submit' existing-seed children support (upstream blocker)
steps.description: Out-of-repo work tracked by warren-d519. Implementation lives in the seeds-cli repo; this step closes once seeds-cli cuts a release containing the capability described in SPEC §11.Q and warren-d519's body. Acceptance: a seeds-cli unit test demonstrates plan submission with existing-seed children (validation: every named seed exists in the same .seeds/issues.jsonl, cross-repo ids rejected, closed seeds rejected at submit time); the released plan row is byte-compatible with today's plan rows for warren's showPlan reader.

steps.title: Double-pin seeds-cli to the existing-seed-children release
steps.description: Once step 1 ships, bump @os-eco/seeds-cli in three locations in lockstep per the burrow-cli rule (CLAUDE.md 'Relationship to burrow', mx-ca1365): package.json dependency, bun.lock (via bun install), Dockerfile global install layer. Quality gates: bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck. CHANGELOG entry names warren-d519 as the unblocker.

steps.title: Warren-side synthesis module + POST /plot-plan-runs endpoint
steps.description: Land src/plot-plan-runs/ (synthesizer + handler glue) per SPEC §11.Q. Synthesizer reads the Plot's seeds_issue attachments, filters out isSdPlanAttachment shapes and closed seeds, mints a throwaway parent seed via sd plan submit --existing-seeds, then calls the existing POST /plan-runs handler in-process with plot_id set. Typed 4xx errors mirror §11.O/§11.P/§11.Q gates: ProjectLacksPlotError, project-without-.seeds error, NoDispatchableSeedsError, malformed plot_id (warren-bae5). Unit tests cover the synthesis happy path + every typed error. Quality gates green.

steps.title: PlotDetail 'Dispatch as plan-run' button
steps.description: Wire the UI on top of POST /plot-plan-runs (src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx). Button renders in the Summary slot next to BatchDispatchAllButton (warren-7c3f), gated on the same isBatchDispatchTarget filter producing ≥1 candidate. Click opens a confirm dialog (shared shape with BatchDispatchDialog) showing the synthesized plan title + the candidate seed list + the closed/sd_plan filter rationale, fires POST /plot-plan-runs, routes to /plan-runs/:id on success. Update BatchDispatchAllButton's inline help text to point at the plan-run alternative as the recommended path. ml record the new component pattern under the ui domain.

steps.title: Acceptance scenario 30: plot-plan-run synthesis roundtrip
steps.description: Land scripts/acceptance/scenarios/30-plot-plan-run-synthesis.ts per SPEC §11.Q acceptance section. Composes scenarios 25 + 27 + 29: real warren+burrow stack, project with .plot/ + .seeds/, Plot with ≥3 seeds_issue attachments (mixed open + closed + sd_plan-shaped). Asserts: synthesized plan row exists; children are open non-sd_plan attachments only; one plan_run_dispatched event on the Plot at start; one run_dispatched per child (including trivial-merge); PLOT_ID + PLOT_ACTOR reach each child sandbox; Plot auto-transitions to 'done' on final merge; all four negative paths return typed 4xx; re-dispatch mints a second PlanRun without clobber. Deterministic, idempotent, cleans up. Quality gates green.

risks: Step 1 is out-of-repo and out-of-this-team's-direct-control (seeds-cli repo). Mitigation: warren-d519 carries the full contract upfront so seeds-cli implementer has a complete spec; this design-lock commit publishes the contract in SPEC §11.Q so cross-repo readers have a single canonical reference.

Step 2's bun.lock bump can churn unrelated transitive deps. Mitigation: scope the lockfile diff review to the seeds-cli line + immediate transitives; if surprises appear, file follow-up rather than expanding step 2's scope.

Step 3's synthesis step shells out to sd plan submit — same Bun.spawn pattern as existing handlers, but a new caller. Mitigation: unit test against a temp .seeds/ fixture, and isolate the sd invocation behind a stub-injected dep bag mirroring src/plan-runs/coordinator.ts's pattern (mx-985743) so the handler test can run hermetically.

Step 4's confirm-dialog shape needs to convey both the synthesis filter rationale (closed + sd_plan exclusions) AND the candidate seed list compactly. Mitigation: lift the candidate-list rendering from BatchDispatchDialog and add a filter-rationale subheader; if the dialog becomes load-bearing, extract a shared dialog component in a follow-up.

Step 5's scenario 30 composes three existing scenarios; flakes in 25/27/29 will compound. Mitigation: rerun the composed scenarios in CI head before adding 30 to confirm baseline green; gate 30 on the same Linux-only matrix as 27 if Plot env-injection assertions need it.
acceptance: Step 1 (warren-d519) closed when seeds-cli ships a release with existing-seed children support and a passing unit test in the seeds-cli repo.
Step 2 closed when bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck pass on the lockstep bump and CHANGELOG names warren-d519.

Step 3 closed when POST /plot-plan-runs returns 201 + a real PlanRun row on the happy path, all four typed error paths return the documented 4xx shapes, unit tests cover both, and quality gates green.

Step 4 closed when PlotDetail's new button dispatches end-to-end against a plot-3e72876d-shaped Plot (≥5 seeds_issue attachments → 1 PlanRun with N children → /plan-runs/:id navigation on success) and the confirm dialog renders correctly with mixed open/closed/sd_plan inputs.
Step 5 (scenario 30) closed when the scenario passes against a live warren+burrow stack and is wired into the acceptance harness alongside scenarios 27 + 29.
Sub-plan closed when warren-a4b7 itself closes — i.e., all five steps merged with no regression in pl-7937 / pl-a258 / pl-5310 steps 1-3.

sections.context: Warren's Plot integration is technically complete (SPEC §11.O Phases 1-3 shipped: env injection, event mirroring, ACL-narrowed write facade, /plots list + detail UI, PlanRun plot_id threading). But actual adoption is zero. The wiring exists; the loop does not close inside warren. The user's current workflow: open repo locally → brainstorm with an agent → write sd plan → commit & push → dispatch agents in warren → rebase & pull merged work → repeat. Warren is one bounded step in a loop the user spends most of their time outside of. Three concrete adoption blockers: (a) the dispatch flow starts at New Run, not at a Plot — Plots are bypassed by habit; (b) nothing pulls the user back to a Plot once dispatched — agents emit events into a passive log no one watches; (c) the mental model is fuzzy — when is work big enough for a Plot vs just a Run? This plan collapses the local-repo loop into warren end-to-end: a Plot is where you brainstorm, plan, dispatch, get pinged with questions, review, and merge. The local editor exists only when you choose hands-on coding. V1 ships the conversational + signal primitives that make Plot the place you live. V1.5 ships the repo-management surface that completes the 'never git pull again' promise.
sections.approach: Build one new run mode — interactive runs — that's respawn-per-turn, Plot-context-loaded, and configurable on agent/tools/scope. The same primitive powers three distinct user-facing features: brainstorming (sharpen an unformed idea into Plot intent), planning (Plot → sd plan submission as a built-in agent), and any future Plot-context conversational use case (answer-question, debug-with-agent, PR-review chat). Building once and configuring three ways is architecturally cheaper than three independent features and makes the fourth interactive use case free. Pair this with blocking question_posed for batch runs: when an in-flight batch run emits question_posed via plot append, the warren supervisor detects it on its event-file poll, transitions the run to a new `paused` state, and waits for a `question_answered` event on the same Plot. A configurable per-project timeout (default 30 min, configurable in .warren/config.yaml) bounds wait time — on timeout, the run respawns with state lost (agent re-reads .plot/ to recover). Surface Plots-needing-attention via a new GET /plots?filter=needs_attention endpoint and a 'Needs you' chip on the Plots page, plus a sidebar badge. Default the home page to /plots when any project has .plot/. The interactive-run sandbox model uses existing burrow respawn machinery — no burrow contract change. The brainstorm session IS a draft Plot from minute zero (status=drafting, empty intent, messages persisted as Plot events); 'Formalize' is a button that runs a summarize turn, suggests intent, user edits, transitions to ready. The planner agent is a real built-in registered alongside claude-code/sapling, with a sandbox config that allows writes to .plot/ and .seeds/ but blocks source-code writes. V1.5 layers click-to-merge on top: a Merge button on gh_pr attachments calls the GitHub API to merge, then schedules a project refresh that pulls the merge into the local clone. PR review still happens on GitHub (deferred native diff UI to V2); the user-facing promise is they never run git pull for the common loop. A done Plot renders as a clean readable summary at /plots/:id/summary (intent, decisions filtered from event log, linked PRs, timeline) — institutional memory artifact.

alternatives.name: Build brainstorm + planner + answer-question as independent features
alternatives.rejected_because: Three independent code paths, 3-4× the surface area to maintain, and adding the fourth interactive use case later (PR review chat, debug-with-agent) becomes its own engineering ask. Unified interactive-run primitive collapses these into one configurable thing and makes new uses free.

alternatives.name: Warm sandboxes for interactive runs (containers stay alive between turns)
alternatives.rejected_because: Better per-turn UX (no cold start, in-memory scout state preserved) but requires a deep burrow contract change (idle-aware reaper, hibernation primitive, resource accounting for live-but-idle containers). Respawn-per-turn ships V1 with zero burrow changes; revisit in V2 once usage justifies the cost.

alternatives.name: Native diff/review UI in warren for the 'never git pull again' promise
alternatives.rejected_because: Eliminates GitHub context-switch entirely but is months of work (diff viewer, comment threads, approval UI, mergeable state machine). V1.5 click-to-merge + auto-sync delivers the user-facing promise (no manual git pull) at ~5% of the cost by keeping review on GitHub.

alternatives.name: Webhook-triggered Plot creation from labeled GitHub issues
alternatives.rejected_because: Powerful adoption vector but couples to SPEC §11.I V2 trigger work that's already deferred. Manual Plot creation (brainstorm-first or blank-canvas) needs to be well-understood first; webhook auto-creation is V2.

alternatives.name: True burrow pause/resume primitive for blocking question_posed
alternatives.rejected_because: Cleanest UX (agent retains in-memory state across pause) but requires burrow-side hibernation work + warren-side resume orchestration. Timeout-respawn (state lost, agent re-reads .plot/ to recover) is good enough for V1 and uses zero new infra.

steps.title: DB schema: runs.mode column (batch|interactive default batch), pause columns (paused_at, paused_question_event_id), add 'paused' to RUN_STATES, extend ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS (running→paused, paused→running, paused→cancelled). Mirror sqlite + postgres in src/db/schema/, update drift.test.ts, add migration.

Project config: extend DefaultsConfigSchema in src/warren-config/schema.ts with agent.pauseTimeoutMs (default 1800000 = 30 min). Surface via loadWarrenConfig(). Document in SPEC §11.O and warren CLAUDE.md.

Interactive run primitive (server): src/runs/interactive.ts implements respawn-per-turn lifecycle (spawnInteractiveTurn(runId, message) reads Plot context — intent + last N events + attachments — constructs prompt, spawns burrow via existing spawnRun, captures reply on reap). Persist user/agent messages in events table with new kinds (user_message, agent_message). Composable with interactiveAgent config (brainstorm vs planner).

HTTP API: extend POST /runs to accept mode='interactive' + interactiveAgent + plot_id (required for interactive). Add POST /runs/:id/messages (send user turn, returns 202). Extend GET /runs/:id/events to stream interactive message events. Update handlers in src/server/handlers.ts; add typed request/response schemas.

Blocking question_posed for batch runs: supervisor (src/supervisor/main.ts) polls .plot/plot-*.events.jsonl for new question_posed events on in-flight batch runs (use .plot/.index.db, not raw JSONL). On detection: transition run to 'paused', persist paused_question_event_id, schedule pauseTimeoutMs. On question_answered for same Plot+question: respawn agent turn with answer in prompt context (state lost; agent re-reads .plot/). On timeout: same respawn with timeout warning. Tests in src/runs/pause.test.ts covering pause detection, resume on answer, timeout.

Built-in agent: brainstorm. New file src/registry/builtins/brainstorm.ts. System prompt: helps user sharpen an unformed idea into Plot intent. Burrow config: read-only scout (rg, read files, web fetch); no plot writes, no source-code writes, no dispatch. Register in src/registry/builtins/index.ts seedBuiltinAgents(). Fixture tests.

Built-in agent: planner. New file src/registry/builtins/planner.ts. System prompt: reads Plot intent, scouts repo, asks clarifying questions interactively, submits structured sd plan via sd plan submit, attaches resulting seeds to Plot. Burrow config: read-only scout + writes restricted to .plot/ and .seeds/ paths; no source-code writes. Register. Fixture tests.

Brainstorm Plot creation flow: POST /brainstorm endpoint creates a draft Plot (status=drafting, empty intent, auto-named) and spawns first interactive turn with brainstorm agent. POST /plots/:id/formalize runs a summarize turn that returns suggested intent (goal, non_goals, constraints, success_criteria) from conversation; user edits via existing POST /plots/:id/intent, transitions to ready via existing status endpoint. Tests cover draft-from-zero, formalize roundtrip.

Plot needs-attention API: extend GET /plots with filter=needs_attention returning Plots with (a) paused runs awaiting answer, (b) gh_pr attachments merged but not reviewed, (c) draft Plots with no activity in N days. New PlotsRepo.listNeedsAttention() method. Counts endpoint GET /plots/needs-attention/count for sidebar badge.

UI chat component primitive: src/ui/src/components/Chat.tsx renders streaming message list (user + agent bubbles), input box, send button. Consumes useEventStream for live agent replies via /runs/:id/events. Reusable across brainstorm + planner + future interactive surfaces. Install shadcn ScrollArea + Tabs if needed.

UI Plot detail interactive surfaces: extend src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx with 'Start brainstorming' button (creates Plot via POST /brainstorm if none, else opens chat), 'Run planner' button (spawns planner interactive run), inline Chat panel for currently-active interactive run, 'Formalize' button (POST /plots/:id/formalize → shows suggested intent for user review → transitions to ready on accept).

UI paused-run surfacing: extend PlotDetail activity feed to render question_posed events with prominent 'Answer & resume' card (textarea + submit calling existing POST /plots/:id/questions/:event_id/answer). Show countdown to pause timeout. StateBadge variant for 'paused' run state.

UI Plot-first home + inbox: modify src/ui/src/components/DefaultLanding.tsx to redirect / → /plots unconditionally when any project has hasPlot. Add 'Needs you' filter chip to Plots.tsx (queries ?filter=needs_attention). Add sidebar badge in Layout.tsx counting needs-attention Plots. 'Start brainstorming' + 'New Plot' primary buttons at top of /plots.

(V1.5) Click-to-merge + auto-sync: POST /plots/:id/attachments/:ref/merge endpoint calls GitHub API to merge gh_pr attachment. On success, schedule refreshProjectClone() to pull merged commits into local clone. Surface merge state + GitHub rate-limit/error states in PR attachment UI in PlotDetail. Background sync notifies via existing event stream.

(V1.5) Plot summary artifact view: GET /plots/:id/summary endpoint returns curated payload (formatted intent, decisions filtered from event log by type=decision_made, linked PRs + commits, timeline). New page src/ui/src/pages/PlotSummary.tsx at /plots/:id/summary. Clean readable layout suitable as institutional memory artifact.

Acceptance harness scenario: scripts/acceptance/scenarios/26-plot-workbench-loop.ts covers full V1+V1.5 loop end-to-end against live warren+burrow: create brainstorm Plot, chat, formalize, run planner, submit sd plan, dispatch PlanRun, agent emits question_posed, run pauses, user answers via API, run resumes, PR opens, user clicks merge, auto-sync pulls into clone, Plot auto-transitions to done, summary renders. Idempotent, deterministic, cleans up.

risks: Respawn-per-turn interactive runs lose in-memory scout state between user messages, which may produce inconsistent reasoning across turns. Mitigated by Plot context (intent + recent events + attachments) carrying state through each prompt. If quality suffers in practice, escalates V2 warm-sandbox priority.

Batch run pause/resume on timeout loses agent in-memory state (open file handles, partial scouts, mid-LLM-call). First turn after resume must re-scout from .plot/ state, costing tokens and time. Document explicitly in agent system prompts so the agent recovers gracefully.
Brainstorm 'Formalize' may produce poor intent suggestions for genuinely half-formed ideas. Treat as a draft the user edits — never auto-commit suggested intent to the Plot.

Planner agent's sd plan submit is destructive (spawns child seeds permanently). Needs UX gate in chat: planner shows the proposed plan, user clicks 'Submit plan' to confirm, only then does sd plan submit run. No auto-submit.

.plot/plot-*.events.jsonl polling by the supervisor could become slow on Plots with thousands of events. Must use the .plot/.index.db SQLite index (rebuildable, gitignored) that plot CLI already maintains — not raw JSONL scans. Add reindex hook on supervisor startup.

Click-to-merge couples warren to GitHub API quotas; rate limits could block merges during heavy use. Reuse existing GitHub client with retry/backoff; surface 429s clearly with 'retry in N seconds' UI affordance.

Auto-sync after merge could race with in-flight local edits in the user's clone. Refresh must detect a dirty working tree and skip the auto-pull, surfacing a 'manual sync needed' state on the project rather than overwriting work.

Interactive run + batch run sharing the same Plot could race on .plot/ writes (agent mid-interactive-run while batch run also appends). Existing append-only merge semantics handle the JSONL case idempotently; .plot/<id>.json full-doc writes may conflict — surface plot.conflict events to user (currently silent).

Brainstorm/planner agents leaking into source-code writes despite sandbox config (e.g. via an unanticipated tool path). Defense: integration tests assert the sandbox config blocks writes outside .plot/ + .seeds/ for both agents; acceptance scenario verifies via filesystem snapshot.
acceptance: A user can open warren, click 'Start brainstorming' from /plots, chat with an agent that has read-only scout access, see the chat persist as plot events on a draft Plot, click 'Formalize', review agent-suggested intent, edit, and transition the Plot to ready — all without leaving the browser.

A user can click 'Run planner' on a ready Plot, answer the planner's clarifying questions in chat, see an sd plan submitted with child seeds attached as Plot attachments, and dispatch the resulting PlanRun with one click.

When a batch run inside a PlanRun emits plot append question_posed, the run transitions to 'paused' within 5s of the supervisor's next poll, the Plot UI shows the question with a prominent 'Answer & resume' card, the run resumes within 5s of the user submitting an answer, and times out gracefully after the configured pauseTimeoutMs (default 30 min) without leaving an orphaned burrow container.

The warren home page at / redirects to /plots whenever any project has .plot/, /plots renders a 'Needs you' filter chip showing Plots with paused runs / unanswered questions / merged-but-unreviewed PRs, and the sidebar shows a badge with the needs-attention count that updates within 30s of state change.

A user can click 'Merge' on a gh_pr attachment in a Plot; warren merges via the GitHub API and the merged commits appear in the local project clone within 30s without the user running git pull. Failure modes (auth error, mergeability conflict, GitHub 429) surface clearly in the UI.

A done Plot renders at /plots/:id/summary as a readable institutional-memory artifact showing formatted intent, decisions (filtered from event log), linked PRs + commits, and a timeline — suitable to share with a teammate as the record of what happened and why.

Brainstorm and planner agents cannot write to source-code paths even via unanticipated tool paths — integration tests verify sandbox config blocks writes outside .plot/ + .seeds/, and the acceptance scenario verifies via post-run filesystem snapshot that no source files were touched.

Acceptance scenario scripts/acceptance/scenarios/26-plot-workbench-loop.ts passes end-to-end against a live warren+burrow stack, exercising brainstorm → formalize → planner → submit → dispatch → pause → answer → resume → merge → auto-sync → done → summary in one deterministic, idempotent run that cleans up after itself.
bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck pass on the merged result of all child seeds.

name: Plot as workbench: interactive runs + blocking question_posed + warren-only loop

sections.context: Currently, the Warren UI is built primarily for desktop-sized viewports. The navigation sidebar is completely hidden on viewports smaller than 'md', leaving mobile users with absolutely no way to navigate between pages (Runs, Projects, Agents, Plans, and Plots). Furthermore, wide tables and complex multi-column grid layouts overflow or get squished on small screens, and touch targets are not optimized for mobile interaction. To make Warren fully usable on mobile, we need responsive navigation overlays, proper viewport scaling, horizontal overflow handling, and touch target adjustments without changing any backend code or major desktop UI aesthetics.
sections.approach: We will implement a responsive layout wrapper. On viewports smaller than 'md', a top header with a hamburger menu button will be displayed. This hamburger button will toggle a mobile navigation drawer overlay (either using Radix UI's Dialog primitive customized as a slide-over panel, or a highly responsive custom React overlay) that replicates the sidebar options. We will also update the main container padding to scale down on small viewports and audit each page to ensure tables scroll nicely, multi-column grids collapse to a single column on mobile, and form controls have adequate touch heights.

alternatives.name: A tab bar navigation at the bottom of the screen
alternatives.rejected_because: This would differ significantly from the desktop navigation hierarchy and sidebar order, increasing maintenance complexity and deviating from the design goal of keeping desktop aesthetics intact.

alternatives.name: Completely rebuilding tables as card lists on mobile
alternatives.rejected_because: Standard tables with horizontal scrolling/truncation are cleaner, simpler, and satisfy the mobile usability requirement with minimal complexity and code duplication.

steps.title: Implement mobile navigation shell and responsive layout wrapper
steps.description: Create a MobileHeader component visible on viewports < md. Implement a mobile drawer containing all sidebar links (Plots, Runs, Plans, Projects, Agents), the 'Dispatch run' button, theme toggle, and log-out button. Wire the drawer to automatically close when a navigation link is clicked. Update main padding to p-4 on mobile and p-6/8 on md+.

steps.title: Enhance Runs, Plan-Runs, and Projects table pages responsiveness
steps.description: Ensure runs lists, plan runs, and projects lists tables handle horizontal overflow correctly. Truncate or hide less-critical columns (e.g., started date, long project urls, costs) on xs/sm screens using CSS/Tailwind utilities. Prevent filter pills from causing page stretching by allowing them to wrap properly.

steps.title: Ensure Plots list and Plot details pages wrap and display correctly
steps.description: Optimize the three-panel PlotDetailPage layout for mobile by allowing grid-cols-2/3 layouts to stack vertically (grid-cols-1) on smaller screens. Adjust ActivityFeed event grouping to display cleanly with reduced horizontal margins, and ensure transition buttons wrap and stack neatly.

steps.title: Optimize form pages (New Run, New Plan Run) and touch targets
steps.description: Ensure form textareas, inputs, and buttons are responsive. Increase spacing and ensure touch target sizes are at least 44px for a great mobile experience. Make sure the prompt pre-formatted blocks wrap instead of overflowing pages.

risks: Drawer trigger state getting out of sync when the user resizes their browser window from mobile to desktop. Fix: Use Tailwind's hidden classes on the mobile header and drawer, and ensure the desktop sidebar remains hidden/visible strictly based on breakpoints.

Navigating with HashRouter doesn't trigger full page reloads, so clicking a menu item needs to explicitly close the mobile drawer. Fix: Pass a close handler to the NavLink click handlers in the mobile drawer.
acceptance: The Warren UI has a mobile-friendly layout and navigation menu accessible on viewports < md (tested down to 320px).
All navigation links in the mobile menu function correctly, navigate without page refresh, and close the drawer upon selection.
No backend code is altered during these enhancements.
No desktop UI experience is altered or degraded.

name: Improve mobile UX in Warren UI

sections.context: Warren's UI is functional but feels half-baked and clunky. Several pages have incomplete implementations: the runs list is hard-capped at 100 with no pagination, cost tracking only reflects the last 100 runs, plot interactive chat triggers agents that never write back, plot names can't be edited, plot status can only move forward, and plan-runs lack cost visibility. There's also no centralized cost analytics view. These gaps make the UI unintuitive and prevent warren from feeling like a production-quality control plane.
sections.approach: Address each issue individually as independent tasks. Start with the runs table and cost infrastructure since they're foundational, then fix plot UX bugs, then build out cost analytics. Each step is scoped to be completable in a single session.

alternatives.name: Full UI rewrite
alternatives.rejected_because: Too much scope; incremental fixes are better

alternatives.name: Wait for v2
alternatives.rejected_because: These are quality-of-life issues that hurt daily usage now

steps.title: Runs page: replace truncated list with paginated, sortable table
steps.description: The /runs/ page hard-caps at 100 items with no way to access older runs. Replace the current list with a proper table component supporting pagination, column sorting, and configurable page size. The API query should support offset/limit or cursor-based pagination. This also impacts cost tracking — the cost summary currently only reflects the last 100 runs rather than all-time totals.

steps.title: Fix or remove plot interactive chat
steps.description: The interactive chat on the plots page is broken: sending a prompt triggers an agent but the agent never writes back to the plot. The current data flow (every message = 1 new GitHub branch & PR) is fundamentally flawed. Options: (1) remove the interactive chat entirely until we have proper agent re-wake support and a better data strategy, or (2) fix the write-back so agent responses appear in the plot event log. Recommend option 1 — remove or clearly mark as experimental — since the underlying architecture needs rethinking.

steps.title: Allow renaming plots
steps.description: There is currently no way to change the name of a plot after creation. Add an editable title field or rename action to the plot detail page. A corresponding seed has been filed in the plot sub-repo for the data-layer side.

steps.title: Plot status: replace forward-only button with dropdown selector
steps.description: Plot status can currently only move forward through phases via a single button. Replace this with a dropdown selector that allows moving status both forward and backward. Users need to be able to revert a plot to a previous phase when circumstances change.

steps.title: Show cost per plan on plan-runs page
steps.description: The /plan-runs/ page currently shows no cost information. Add a cost column or summary showing the aggregate cost of all child runs within each plan-run. This requires summing costs from the individual runs that belong to each plan execution.

steps.title: Build a cost analytics view
steps.description: Add a dedicated costs page (or modal/popup) that provides cost breakdown by multiple dimensions: date, project, plan, plot, run, agent, model, and provider. This is the foundation for agentic cost analysis — understanding spend patterns across the system. Should support filtering and grouping by any combination of these dimensions. Consider a simple table with filter dropdowns initially, with charts as a follow-up.

risks: Runs pagination requires API changes that could break existing consumers
Removing interactive chat may frustrate users who rely on it despite its bugs
Cost analytics requires consistent cost tracking across all run types — gaps in data will produce misleading totals
acceptance: Runs page displays all runs with pagination, sorting, and configurable page size
Cost summary on runs page reflects all-time totals, not just the visible page
Plot interactive chat is either fixed end-to-end or clearly removed/marked experimental
Plot names are editable from the UI
Plot status can be changed to any valid phase via dropdown
Plan-runs page shows aggregate cost per plan
A cost analytics view exists with breakdown by date, project, plan, plot, run, agent, model, and provider

sections.context: When plot metadata is edited in Warren (rename, intent edit, status change, brainstorm formalize), the .plot/ file changes stay as uncommitted working-tree modifications in the project clone. These changes are preserved across refreshes via snapshot/restore, but they never make it into git as durable history. If the warren instance dies or the clone is wiped, plot state is lost. Users expect plot state to be persisted in the repo just like code changes — via PRs that land on the default branch.
sections.approach: A batched 'plot sync' operation rather than per-edit PRs (which would be noisy). The sync detects dirty .plot/ files, creates a git worktree from origin/<target> to avoid touching the main clone's branch state, commits the changes as warren@os-eco.dev, pushes a warren/plot-sync-<hash> branch, opens a PR via the existing openPullRequest function, and optionally merges via mergePullRequest. Auto-triggers fire (fire-and-forget) after status changes and formalize. A manual 'Sync to GitHub' button lives in the PlotDetail UI. Merge strategy is configurable in .warren/config.yaml (immediate / auto / manual). Target branch is also configurable, defaulting to the project's defaultBranch.

alternatives.name: Per-edit PRs
alternatives.rejected_because: Renaming + intent editing + status changes would create dozens of tiny PRs per plot lifecycle — too noisy

alternatives.name: Direct push to main
alternatives.rejected_because: Repos often have branch protection rules that prevent direct pushes

alternatives.name: Branch-switch on project clone
alternatives.rejected_because: Switching branches on the active clone risks interfering with concurrent handler operations reading from the same directory

steps.title: Add PlotSyncConfig to warren-config schema
steps.description: Add PlotSyncConfigSchema to src/warren-config/schema.ts as a strict Zod object with mergeStrategy ('immediate'|'auto'|'manual', default 'immediate') and targetBranch (optional string, defaults to project.defaultBranch). Add plot: PlotSyncConfigSchema.optional() to DefaultsConfigSchema. Re-export from index.ts. Follow the AgentConfigSchema pattern.

steps.title: Implement core plot sync module (src/plots/sync.ts)
steps.description: Create PlotSyncer interface and defaultPlotSyncer factory. Flow: (1) git diff --name-only -- .plot/ + git ls-files --others --exclude-standard -- .plot/ to detect dirty files, (2) git fetch origin <target>, (3) git worktree add -b warren/plot-sync-<hash> <tmpdir> origin/<target>, (4) copy .plot/ files (skip .index.db*) into worktree, (5) git add -- .plot/ + git diff --cached --quiet check, (6) git commit as warren@os-eco.dev, (7) git push origin <branch>, (8) openPullRequest from src/runs/pr.ts, (9) if mergeStrategy=immediate call mergePullRequest then schedule refreshProject, if mergeStrategy=auto enable GitHub auto-merge via API, (10) git worktree remove in finally block. Return { synced, prUrl, merged }. Add PlotSyncer to ServerDeps in src/server/types.ts.

steps.title: Wire server handler and auto-triggers
steps.description: Add POST /plots/:id/sync route to src/server/handlers.ts following the existing plot handler pattern (plotResolver, hasPlot gate, deps.plotSyncer ?? createDefaultPlotSyncer). Load plot sync config from deps.warrenConfigs. In changePlotStatusHandler, after changer.change() succeeds, fire-and-forget deps.plotSyncer.sync(). In formalizePlotHandler, after formalizer.formalize() succeeds, fire-and-forget deps.plotSyncer.sync(). Both auto-triggers log warnings on failure but never block the response.

steps.title: Add UI sync button to PlotDetail
steps.description: Add plotsApi.syncToGitHub(plotId) to src/ui/src/api/client.ts. Add SyncPlotToGitHubResponse type to src/ui/src/api/types.ts. Add a 'Sync to GitHub' button in the SubstratePanel header of PlotDetail.tsx, using the standard useMutation pattern with inline error rendering and disabled-while-pending state. On success, invalidate the plot query cache.

steps.title: Tests and quality gates
steps.description: Write tests for the sync module (src/plots/sync.test.ts) covering: no-op when no dirty files, successful sync flow with mocked git/GitHub, merge strategy variations, worktree cleanup on failure. Run bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck to verify all quality gates pass.

risks: Concurrent syncs for the same project could race on branch names or worktree paths — mitigate with a per-project mutex or unique branch suffixes
Git worktree operations might fail on some environments or older git versions — add graceful error handling and clear error messages
After immediate merge, refreshProject must run to update the clone's HEAD — if refresh fails, next sync might re-sync same changes (idempotent via openPullRequest 422 handling)
acceptance: Renaming a plot, then clicking 'Sync to GitHub' in the UI opens a PR with the .plot/ changes and merges it (when mergeStrategy=immediate)
Changing a plot's status auto-triggers a background sync that opens and merges a PR without blocking the status change response
Formalize auto-triggers a background sync
When no .plot/ files are dirty, sync returns a no-op result and does not create a branch or PR
mergeStrategy config in .warren/config.yaml is respected (immediate/auto/manual)
targetBranch config overrides the default branch when set
All quality gates pass: bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck

sections.context: External projects need to autonomously dispatch coding runs against their own codebases via warren's HTTP API. Currently there is no typed client — callers must hand-roll fetch calls. A proper SDK following the src/burrow-client/ pattern will make warren programmatically accessible.
sections.approach: Create src/client/ with zero server-side deps. Declare wire types fresh (no drizzle, bun:sqlite, burrow imports). WarrenClient class with bearer token auth covers: project/agent management, run dispatch, SSE event streaming, mid-run steering, and plot/plan-run management. Single version — SDK and CLI cannot drift.

steps.title: Define wire types and WarrenClient skeleton
Implement project and agent management endpoints
Implement run dispatch and status polling
Implement SSE event streaming
Implement mid-run steering (interactive prompt injection)
Implement plot and plan-run management
Release v0.6.0 — Warren Client SDK

name: Warren Client SDK — typed remote client for external agents

sections.context: Operators set `connect_timeout` to bound how long warren waits for a preview sidecar's port to bind. When a real dev server (e.g. Next.js) binds quickly but takes much longer than 2s to send its first response headers (mid-compile), every phase-1 probe is aborted by the AbortController and classified as `not_connected`. The phase-1 → phase-2 transition never fires, the connect budget is consumed, and the launch fails with `connect_timeout` even though the port has actually been bound for most of that time. Users hit this on real projects (run_sjxddbjpg950) and have to inflate `connect_timeout` to 30m to work around it. Fixing phase-1 to probe TCP only matches the phase's actual responsibility ("did anything bind on the port?") and restores the original budget split intent (warren-9b15).
sections.reproduction: 1. Configure a preview with a slow-first-headers dev server (e.g. Next.js cold start that reports `Ready in 41s` then enters `Compiling /`). 2. Use the default `connect_timeout` and `probePerCallTimeoutMs` (2s). 3. Dispatch a run whose preview targets that sidecar. 4. Observe in src/preview/launch.ts the phase-1 loop: every call to `probeOnce(fetchImpl, probeUrl, perCallTimeoutMs)` enters the `catch` arm because `ac.abort()` fires before the dev server can send headers; `probe === 'not_connected'`. 5. The loop never breaks out of phase-1 and eventually fails with `reason: 'connect_timeout'` even though the TCP port has been bound the whole time. Expected: phase-1 should observe the bound port within the first second and transition to phase-2 (readiness) where the larger budget applies. Observed: phase-1 misclassifies the bound-but-slow server as never-connected.
sections.root_cause: `probeOnce()` in src/preview/launch.ts (~line 495) does HTTP-level work (`fetchImpl(url, ...)`) for both phases. In phase 1 we only care whether a TCP listener exists, but `fetch` waits for response headers before resolving. If `AbortController` fires before headers arrive, the catch arm returns `not_connected` — indistinguishable in that arm from a real ECONNREFUSED. The launch.ts comment block at lines 478-491 already acknowledges this ambiguity: "We can't perfectly distinguish 'bound but hung mid-headers + abort' from 'never connected + abort' inside the catch arm; treating that case as not_connected is the conservative choice". The fix is to use a probe that answers the actual phase-1 question (is the port accepting TCP?) without doing HTTP work, so the abort/timeout ambiguity goes away.
sections.approach: Split the probe into two functions: (1) a new `tcpConnectOnce(host, port, timeoutMs)` that opens a raw TCP socket using `Bun.connect` (returning `connected` | `not_connected`) and immediately closes it, used only in phase-1; (2) keep the existing `probeOnce` HTTP probe but use it only in phase-2 for readiness (`ready` vs not-ready). Phase-1 transitions to phase-2 the first time `tcpConnectOnce` returns `connected`. This matches the phase semantics already documented in launch.ts (warren-9b15) without changing public API: callers still pass `connect_timeout`, `readiness_path`, and `probePerCallTimeoutMs` exactly as before; the input/output shapes of `attemptPreviewLaunch` are unchanged. Alternative considered: bump `PROBE_PER_CALL_TIMEOUT_MS` only in phase-1. Rejected — it slows down detection of truly dead servers and doesn't remove the underlying ambiguity (a server that accepts TCP and then hangs forever would still get classified wrong eventually). The TCP-connect approach is unambiguous and faster on the happy path.

steps.title: Add tcpConnectOnce helper in src/preview/launch.ts
steps.description: In src/preview/launch.ts, add a new internal helper `tcpConnectOnce(host: string, port: number, timeoutMs: number): Promise<'connected' | 'not_connected'>` that opens a TCP socket via `Bun.connect({ hostname, port, socket: { ... } })`, races it against a `setTimeout`-based timer, closes the socket as soon as `open` fires (we don't need to send any bytes), and returns `'connected'` on open or `'not_connected'` on error/timeout. Place the helper next to `probeOnce` (~line 495) and document with a short docstring noting it is the phase-1 discriminator that replaces the HTTP probe (referencing warren-9b15 and this bug). Do not call it from the launch loop yet — this step is the primitive only. Do not change the existing `probeOnce` signature or behaviour. Make sure errors during `Bun.connect` (refused, EHOSTUNREACH, abort/timeout) all map to `'not_connected'` via a single try/catch + timer cleanup pattern symmetric to the current `probeOnce`. Strict-mode care: handle the Bun socket types without `any`; if the open-handler resolves with a TCPSocket, call `.end()` then `.unref?.()` defensively. Tab indentation, 100-char width.

steps.title: Use tcpConnectOnce in the phase-1 loop and reserve probeOnce for phase-2
steps.description: In src/preview/launch.ts, modify the phase-1 loop (~lines 310-327) so it calls `tcpConnectOnce('127.0.0.1', port, perCallTimeoutMs)` instead of `probeOnce(fetchImpl, probeUrl, perCallTimeoutMs)`. Treat the result as: `'connected'` ⇒ break out of phase-1 into phase-2 (the existing `break` path that the prior `http_response` branch took); `'not_connected'` ⇒ keep waiting under the connect budget exactly as today. Remove the `'ready'`-from-phase-1 shortcut: phase-1 no longer asserts readiness because it does not look at HTTP status. (The first iteration of the phase-2 loop will immediately do an HTTP probe, so any server that is already serving 2xx still completes within one extra poll interval.) Leave phase-2 (~lines 346+) unchanged: it still calls `probeOnce(fetchImpl, probeUrl, perCallTimeoutMs)` and treats `'ready'`/'http_response'/'not_connected' as today. Update the failure message string in the connect_timeout branch (~line 327) to read "preview port did not accept a TCP connection within ${connectTimeoutMs}ms (probed 127.0.0.1:${port})" — it already says "TCP connection" but should no longer reference `probeUrl`. Also update the comment block at lines 478-491 (`ProbeOutcome` doc) to remove the ambiguity caveat about `'not_connected'` and note that phase-1 now uses `tcpConnectOnce` instead. Do not change `ProbeOutcome` itself or any exported names.

steps.title: Add unit tests for tcpConnectOnce and the phase-1 TCP-probe path
steps.description: In src/preview/launch.test.ts, add tests covering: (a) `tcpConnectOnce` returns `'connected'` against a real ephemeral TCP listener (use `Bun.listen` to spin one up on `0.0.0.0:0`, capture the assigned port, call the helper, and tear the listener down); (b) returns `'not_connected'` against a closed port; (c) returns `'not_connected'` when the per-call timeout elapses (start a listener that accepts but never reads — or omit the socket and assert against a port nothing is bound to with a 1ms timeout). Then add a higher-level test in the existing `attemptPreviewLaunch` style: a sidecar whose port binds immediately but whose HTTP layer hangs (fetchImpl returns a Promise that resolves after readinessDeadline-friendly latency) — assert the launch reaches phase-2 (no `connect_timeout`) and ultimately succeeds when fetchImpl eventually returns 200. This is the regression test for the original bug. Reuse existing fixtures and helpers; do not refactor unrelated tests. Tab indentation. Keep tests deterministic (use injected `now()` and `sleep` if present, or short ms-scale timeouts).

steps.title: Release: run /release per .claude/commands/release.md.
steps.description: Run the release flow documented in .claude/commands/release.md to ship the fix. This is always the last step; it runs after all preceding steps have merged.

Existing connect_timeout test (`returns connect_timeout when the port never accepts a connection`) still passes.
New regression test for `slow-first-headers but bound port` reaches phase-2 readiness and ultimately succeeds.
No change to the exported signatures of attemptPreviewLaunch, AttemptPreviewLaunchInput, or AttemptPreviewLaunchResult.

name: TCP-only phase-1 probe in preview launch

sections.context: When users dispatch a plan-run from the dedicated /plan-runs/new page they can customize `promptTemplate` (a Textarea with `{seed_id}` placeholder) before submitting. When users dispatch a plan-run from a Plot's substrate section via the per-attachment 'Run plan' button, the dialog hard-codes `DEFAULT_PROMPT_TEMPLATE = 'work on sd {seed_id}'` and offers no way to edit it. This means users wanting a different prompt (e.g. 'apply the fix from sd {seed_id} per its plan, then run /release') have to leave the Plot and use /plan-runs/new, losing the Plot binding ergonomics. The plot substrate dialog should mirror NewPlanRun's prompt-template UX so both entry points are equally expressive.
sections.reproduction: 1. Open a Plot detail page that has at least one `sd_plan` attachment in its substrate section. 2. Click the per-attachment 'Run plan' button (RunPlanButton in src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx ~line 1088). 3. Observe the RunPlanDialog (~line 1115) shows the prompt template as a read-only ReadOnlyField (~line 1235) with value `work on sd {seed_id}`. 4. There is no Textarea, no setter, and the value passed to `plotsApi.dispatchPlanRun({ promptTemplate: DEFAULT_PROMPT_TEMPLATE, ... })` (~line 1164) cannot be changed from the UI. Expected: an editable Textarea matching NewPlanRun.tsx behaviour (~line 300), with `{seed_id}` placeholder hint, validation that the trimmed value is non-empty, and the trimmed value passed to dispatchPlanRun.
sections.root_cause: RunPlanDialog in src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx was written as a minimal confirm dialog (warren-5d94 / pl-9d6a step 14) that surfaces a read-only triple plus auto-resolved defaults; it deliberately did not include prompt editing. The plan-runs API and plotsApi.dispatchPlanRun already accept an arbitrary `promptTemplate`, so the missing piece is purely UI: a controlled Textarea + setter + non-empty validation, identical in shape to the prompt-template block already implemented in src/ui/src/pages/NewPlanRun.tsx (~lines 299-320).
sections.approach: Add a controlled Textarea to RunPlanDialog that initializes to DEFAULT_PROMPT_TEMPLATE, tracks a `promptTouched` flag for the 'Default.' hint (matching NewPlanRun's pattern at mx-9c6c6d / NewPlanRun.tsx ~line 317), validates that the trimmed value is non-empty, and is the value passed into `plotsApi.dispatchPlanRun`. Disable the Dispatch button when trimmed prompt is empty. Keep all other dialog behaviour (project, agent, plot, hasSeeds checks, error rendering) unchanged. Do not touch RunPlanButton's call sites or RunPlanDialog's prop signature — the dialog stays internally self-contained. No API change: plotsApi.dispatchPlanRun already accepts promptTemplate.

Compute `const trimmedPrompt = promptTemplate.trim();`. Replace the read-only `` block (~lines 1238-1242) with a Textarea-based input identical in shape to NewPlanRun.tsx lines 299-320: a `

sections.context: Nightwatch patrol on 2026-05-26 scanned the repo for inconsistencies, bugs, type-safety, dead code, test gaps, security, and doc drift. Quality gates are green (lint, typecheck, 2088 tests pass). Two small quality issues were found: (1) doc drift in .claude/commands/release.md referencing a `bun run version:bump` script that CLAUDE.md explicitly documents as not existing, and (2) JSON response inconsistency in src/preview/proxy.ts where two response helpers omit `charset=utf-8` from the `content-type` header while every other JSON response in the codebase (jsonResponse in src/server/response.ts, notFoundResponse in src/server/ui.ts) sets `application/json; charset=utf-8`.
sections.behavior_invariant: External behavior must remain identical: warren CLI, HTTP API shapes, preview proxy error envelopes (the JSON body fields `error.code`, `error.message`, `error.hint`), HTTP status codes, and the release workflow's external steps (CHANGELOG/README/CLAUDE.md updates, commit+tag) are unchanged. Only documentation wording and one response-header value (adding `; charset=utf-8`) change. All 2088 existing tests must continue to pass; lint and typecheck must remain clean.
sections.approach: Fix the two findings independently as small, single-PR edits. Step 1 corrects the release command doc to match CLAUDE.md's explicit instruction to edit `package.json` and `src/index.ts` manually. Step 2 routes the two inline `new Response(JSON.stringify(...))` call sites in src/preview/proxy.ts through (or matches the header shape of) the canonical jsonResponse helper so every JSON response in warren emits the same `content-type: application/json; charset=utf-8`. Step 3 cuts a patch release once the fixes land.

steps.title: Fix release command doc drift in .claude/commands/release.md
steps.description:
File: .claude/commands/release.md, step 4.

Problem: step 4 says `Bump version in both package.json and src/index.ts using bun run version:bump <level>`. CLAUDE.md (`## Version Management` section) explicitly states: "There is **no** `bun run version:bump` script in this repo (unlike burrow); edit both files directly." Running the documented command fails (`bun run version:bump` is not present in package.json scripts), so following the slash-command verbatim breaks the release.

Fix: rewrite step 4 to instruct manual edits of `package.json`'s `version` field and `src/index.ts`'s `export const VERSION = "X.Y.Z"` constant, matching CLAUDE.md's wording. Also verify the rest of the release.md steps still match current reality (CHANGELOG.md section move, CLAUDE.md/README.md updates, commit step) and tighten any other phrasing that drifted.

Correct looks like: step 4 reads roughly `Bump version in both package.json ("version" field) and src/index.ts (export const VERSION = "X.Y.Z") — edit both files directly; the release workflow fails if they disagree.` No code changes outside the markdown file.

steps.title: Normalize preview-proxy JSON response content-type to include charset
steps.description:
File: src/preview/proxy.ts, lines ~872-877 (previewError) and ~899-908 (previewUnauthorized).

Problem: both helpers construct `new Response(JSON.stringify(body), { ... headers: { "content-type": "application/json" } })`, omitting the `; charset=utf-8` suffix that every other JSON response in the codebase emits via `jsonResponse` (src/server/response.ts) and `notFoundResponse` (src/server/ui.ts). The bodies contain UTF-8 (error messages, preview hints) but the header doesn't advertise it — a real (small) wire-format inconsistency across call sites.

Fix: change both inline header literals to `"application/json; charset=utf-8"`. The shared `jsonResponse` helper is not exported in a way that's trivially reusable here (preview proxy intentionally lives below the warren handler stack and the WWW-Authenticate header must be preserved in previewUnauthorized), so the minimal correct change is to align the literal string. Do not change response body shape, status codes, or the `www-authenticate` header.

Correct looks like: `grep -rn 'content-type.*application/json' src --include='*.ts' | grep -v '\.test\.ts'` shows every response-side site using `application/json; charset=utf-8` (request-side client headers in src/client, src/burrow-client, src/runs/pr*.ts may keep `application/json` — those are request bodies sent to remote APIs, not response shapes, and are out of scope). Add or extend a small test in src/preview/proxy.test.ts that asserts the content-type on a 4xx preview response includes `charset=utf-8`.

steps.title: Release: run /release per .claude/commands/release.md
steps.description: Cut a patch release covering the two patrol fixes. Follow the (now-corrected) .claude/commands/release.md: bump version manually in package.json and src/index.ts, move [Unreleased] CHANGELOG entries to a new patch section with today's date, update README.md / CLAUDE.md only if stats or command counts shifted (they should not, since these are doc + header changes), and commit. The release workflow auto-tags and publishes.
