sections.behavior_invariant: The exact 23 tests across pickUnansweredQuestion, findAnswerFor, tickPauseDetector, and bootPauseDetector continue to run and pass with identical bodies, assertions, stubs, and fixtures — only their file location and a shared-helper import seam change. pause.ts itself is untouched. No production code path or import of pause.ts changes.

sections.context: Plan-runs are welded to a single project: src/plan-runs/coordinator.ts resolves every child through planRun.projectId, and src/plan-runs/dispatch.ts (createPlanRunSpawn) calls spawnRun({ projectId: planRun.projectId }). A meta-repo (e.g. kota-monorepo: a directory of independent child repos coordinated by a Makefile + stack.lock + one-way contracts sync) wants a cross-repo plan whose children live in the meta-repo's .seeds/ but each execute against a DIFFERENT child repo. Today such a plan would clone the meta-repo into every child's workspace, where the actual code does not live. The default plan-run prompt template is literally 'work on sd {seed_id}' (NewPlanRun.tsx:19), which expects the executor to run `sd show <id>` IN ITS workspace — so a meta-repo seed is invisible to a child-repo executor. The unlock: warren already does all seed reads/writes and plot mirroring HOST-SIDE against the project clone it holds (R-01: spawn + reap call updateExtensions; coordinator calls showSeed host-side), so the agent never needs cross-repo access. We only need to stop conflating 'which repo to clone into the workspace' with 'which host clone owns the seed'.
sections.approach: Adopt a warren-side convention (NO seeds core change): a child seed carries extensions.repo (a project slug or git remote URL) naming its execution repo. Split spawnRun's single projectId into an execution project (the repo cloned into the workspace) and a seedProjectId (the coordination project whose host clone owns the sd update + plot append), defaulting seedProjectId = projectId so every existing single-repo run stays byte-identical. The plan-run coordinator keeps reading/closing seeds and appending plot against planRun.projectId (the meta-repo), while per-child dispatch resolves extensions.repo to a registered execution project and clones THAT. Because the executor's workspace has no .seeds/ for the meta-repo seed, warren inlines the seed title+body into the prompt ({seed_body}). The serial merge-gate model is unchanged: each child = one repo = one PR = one merge gate, which matches the meta-repo's own decompose-into-serial-single-repo-steps philosophy. Routing knowledge lives in the meta-repo's per-project .canopy/ planner role + a custom seeds plan template (per-step repo field), backed by a warren pre-dispatch guardrail — never in executor context.

alternatives.name: Multi-repo burrow workspace (clone a collection of repos into one sandbox)
alternatives.rejected_because: It crosses the warren<->burrow boundary (provision N clones, N git remotes for creds, N branch pushes) and breaks warren's single-repo reap/PR/preview pipeline (one prUrl, one merge gate, one preview). Its only real win is a TRULY atomic cross-repo change — which the meta-repo design (no submodules, no workspaces, contract-mediated coupling) deliberately avoids. Keep it as a documented future escape hatch, not this plan.

alternatives.name: Build 'meta-repo infra' into seeds core (a first-class child-repo attachment field)
alternatives.rejected_because: Seeds keeps Issue.extensions schema-stable on purpose and stays generic; coupling it to one warren usage pattern is wrong. extensions is already free-form per consumer (R-01), so the routing rides existing seeds features with zero seeds code.

alternatives.name: Have the executor agent update the meta-repo seed directly
alternatives.rejected_because: It has no access to the meta-repo workspace, and warren already owns the host-side seed write at dispatch and reap. Mid-run follow-ups, if ever needed, route through Plot (the meta-repo is already the coordination substrate).

steps.title: Add a `repo` key to the warren-namespaced seeds extensions schema. In src/seeds-cli/warren-extensions.ts add an optional `repo: z.string().min(1)` field to WarrenExtensionsSchema (the project slug or git remote URL a seed's work targets) and export a typed reader (e.g. readTargetRepo(extensions): string | undefined). Confirm src/seeds-cli/schema.ts SeedShowIssue.extensions (z.record(z.string(), z.unknown())) already round-trips `extensions.repo` through `sd show --json`; add a parse case proving it. No seeds CLI change — this is the warren-owned namespace only. Verify: `bun test src/seeds-cli/warren-extensions.test.ts src/seeds-cli/show.test.ts` green with new present/absent/empty-string cases; `bun run typecheck` clean.

Add a repo->project resolver. New src/projects/resolve-target.ts exporting resolveTargetProject(repos, repoRef): Promise<string> that maps a seed's extensions.repo (a project slug/name OR a git remote URL — normalize trailing `.git`, and ssh `git@host:owner/repo` vs https `https://host/owner/repo` forms) to a registered project id via repos.projects, throwing a typed TargetProjectUnresolvedError (joins the existing warren error family in the projects module) when nothing matches. Pure lookup, no clone, no side effects. Verify: `bun test src/projects/resolve-target.test.ts` covers slug match, https URL match, ssh URL match, trailing-.git normalization, and unresolved -> typed error; `bun run typecheck` clean.

Split spawnRun's single project identity into execution vs coordination. In src/runs/spawn/ add an optional `seedProjectId` input distinct from `projectId`: `projectId` selects the repo cloned into the workspace (execution); `seedProjectId` (default = projectId) selects the host project clone used for the post-dispatch seeds updateExtensions write and any plot append/mirror. Thread it so the seed stamp (role/lastRunId/lastRunAt) and plot operations target seedProjectId, never the execution project. Do NOT change the burrow provisioning path. Verify: existing `bun test src/runs/` stays green and byte-identical for the default (seedProjectId === projectId) case; a new test asserts that when seedProjectId != projectId the sd update runs against seedProjectId while the workspace clones projectId; `bun run typecheck` clean.

Inline seed content into the dispatch prompt so a cross-repo executor needs no in-workspace .seeds/ access. Resolve the seed title+body host-side (seeds-cli `sd show` against seedProjectId) and add a `{seed_body}` substitution alongside the existing `{seed_id}` in the plan-run/run prompt builder; when seedProjectId != execution projectId, auto-inject the resolved title+body even if the template only references `{seed_id}`, so the executor receives the issue text directly. Keep `{seed_id}`-only behavior unchanged when the projects match. Verify: unit tests cover `{seed_body}` substitution and the auto-inject-on-divergent-project path; `bun test` for the prompt builder green; `bun run typecheck` clean.

Wire per-child execution-project routing into the plan-run coordinator + dispatch. In src/plan-runs/dispatch.ts createPlanRunSpawn: for each child read extensions.repo from the seed (showSeed already returns extensions), resolve it to an execution project via resolveTargetProject (fallback to planRun.projectId when the tag is absent), and call spawnRun with projectId = resolved execution project AND seedProjectId = planRun.projectId; load the execution project to derive ref/defaultBranch. Keep coordinator.ts showSeed/close/skip and the plot-append pointed at planRun.projectId (coordination). The merge gate is unchanged (polls the child run's prUrl). Verify: `bun test src/plan-runs/` green incl. new cases for (a) routed child clones the child repo while seed reads hit the meta-repo, (b) untagged child falls back to planRun.projectId, (c) unresolved repo fails that child with a typed reason via the existing plan_failed path; `bun run typecheck` clean.

Persist and surface each plan-run child's execution project. Add a nullable execution_project_id column to plan_run_children in BOTH src/db/schema/sqlite.ts and src/db/schema/postgres.ts (+ the shared constant in src/db/schema/columns.ts), generate migrations for both dialects via the drizzle.config.sqlite.ts / drizzle.config.postgres.ts flows (update both meta/_journal.json), have the coordinator write the resolved id when it dispatches a child, expose it on the plan-run detail API response (ROUTE_TABLE), and render a per-child repo column/badge in src/ui/src/pages/PlanRunDetail.tsx (+ src/ui/src/pages/plan-run-detail/). Regenerate docs/openapi.yaml via `bun run gen:openapi`. Verify: `bun run typecheck`, `bun test`, `bun run gen:openapi:check`, and `bun run build:ui` all green; both dialect journals updated.

Add a pre-dispatch guardrail + doctor check for cross-repo plans. At POST /plan-runs (and/or the first coordinator dispatch) validate that every child carrying extensions.repo resolves to a registered project via resolveTargetProject; reject with a structured 4xx listing the unresolved repo refs rather than dispatching into the wrong workspace. Add a `warren doctor` / readyz `cross_repo_plan_targets` check that walks open plans' children and flags unresolved repo tags. Verify: `bun test` covers reject-on-unresolved + happy path through the handler; the doctor check appears in `warren doctor` output; `bun run typecheck` clean.

Generalize routing to the single-run path. In the POST /runs dispatch handler, when the request carries a seedId, read the seed's extensions.repo and resolve the execution project via the shared resolver (fallback to the request's projectId), passing seedProjectId = the seed's home project and using the {seed_body} prompt injection. Keep the no-seedId / same-project path byte-identical. Verify: `bun test` for the runs handler covers seed-routed dispatch and the unchanged default path; `bun run typecheck` clean.

Author the meta-repo planning surface and document the coordination/execution model. Ship an example cross-repo-planner canopy role for the per-project .canopy/ tier whose system prompt encodes 'one plan step = one child repo, tag each with extensions.repo: <slug>' plus the valid child slugs, AND a custom seeds plan template (plan_templates) with a per-step `repo` field so the planner fills a structured slot instead of remembering. Place examples under docs/ (or a templates dir). Update SPEC (the plan-run section, §11.P) + CLAUDE.md/ROADMAP R-20 with the coordination-project-vs-execution-project contract and the 'no seeds core change' decision. Verify: `bun run gen:docs:check`, `bun run check:agents`, and `bun run lint` green; every referenced path exists.

Add an end-to-end acceptance scenario + freeze the contract. New scripts/acceptance/scenarios/NN-cross-repo-plan-run.ts that registers a meta-repo coordination project plus 2 child execution projects, submits a 2-child plan whose children are tagged extensions.repo, dispatches the plan-run, and asserts: each child clones its tagged repo, opens its own PR, the meta-repo seed is updated host-side (role/lastRunId), and serial merge gating advances child 2 only after child 1's PR merges. Deterministic, idempotent, self-cleaning per scripts/acceptance/lib conventions. Verify: the scenario passes against a live warren+burrow stack; `bun run check:all` fully green.

risks: Executor cannot update the meta-repo seed mid-run (its workspace has no .seeds/ for it). Mitigation: warren owns the host-side write at dispatch and reap; mid-run follow-ups route through Plot, not the agent. Documented as an explicit limitation in step 9.

Migration drift between SQLite and Postgres for the new execution_project_id column (step 6). Mitigation: generate and commit both dialect migrations + journals in the same step; check:all runs ci-parity and the pg acceptance path.
Repo-ref normalization ambiguity (slug vs https vs ssh, trailing .git). Mitigation: the resolver (step 2) has explicit table-driven tests for each form before any caller depends on it.

Bundle-size / openapi gates trip on the step 6 UI + API surface. Mitigation: step 6 verification explicitly runs gen:openapi:check and build:ui; re-baseline bundle via `bun run check:bundle-size --update` only if a legitimate within-cap growth.

Conflating execution vs coordination project could regress ordinary single-repo runs. Mitigation: seedProjectId defaults to projectId; every foundational step asserts the default path is byte-identical.
acceptance: A plan whose children are tagged extensions.repo dispatches each child against its tagged registered project's workspace while reading/closing the seed and appending plot against the meta-repo (planRun.projectId) host-side.
An untagged plan child still dispatches against planRun.projectId exactly as today (no behavior change for existing single-repo plan-runs).
A child tagged with an unresolved repo is rejected pre-dispatch (or fails that child with a typed reason) rather than cloning the wrong repo, and the condition is surfaced by a warren doctor check.
A cross-repo executor receives the seed title+body inline in its prompt and completes work without any in-workspace `sd show` of the meta-repo seed.
Serial merge gating is preserved: child N+1 dispatches only after child N's PR merges, across different repos.
`bun run check:all` is fully green and an end-to-end acceptance scenario exercises the meta-repo + 2-child-repo flow.

sections.context: Seeds plans flip to the `approved` status once a human signs off, but today the only way to launch them is the manual /plan-runs/new form where the operator must already know the plan id. There is no surface that proactively shows which approved plans are sitting un-dispatched. This adds a safe, operator-gated 'Ready to dispatch' tab that lists approved plans with at least one open child and no existing plan-run, each with a one-click dispatch that reuses the existing POST /plan-runs path and dispatch popup. True auto-dispatch (Approach A) is explicitly out of scope.
sections.approach: Read-on-demand, no background dispatcher. A new per-project read endpoint GET /projects/:id/ready-plans composes existing seeds-cli readers (sd plan list / sd plan show / sd list) plus a new plan-runs dedup query to compute readiness: status === 'approved', at least one open (non-closed) child seed, and no plan_run row for that plan id. The UI adds a 'Ready to dispatch' tab to the existing Plan runs page that polls this endpoint and opens a generalized version of the existing dispatch dialog (pre-filled plan id + project, model/provider/prompt editable). Dispatch still goes over the unchanged planRunsApi.create (POST /plan-runs); no new dispatch path is introduced, so the operator-gated SPEC §10.4 approval signal is preserved.

alternatives.name: Background poller that auto-dispatches approved plans (Approach A)
alternatives.rejected_because: Dangerous and against the v1 operator-gated taste signal; needs durable dedup + default agent/model/prompt with no human in the loop. Deferred as an opt-in layer that can reuse this surface's readiness logic later.

alternatives.name: Dedicated new top-level Plans page
alternatives.rejected_because: Heavier nav surface for a feature that is conceptually a pre-stage of plan runs; a tab on the existing Plan runs page keeps it discoverable next to the runs it produces.

steps.title: Add PlanRunsRepo.listDispatchedPlanIds(projectId) returning the distinct set of plan ids that already have a plan_run row for the project (the dedup primitive). Implement as a SELECT DISTINCT plan_id WHERE project_id = ? returning string[]. Add unit tests in src/db/repos/plan-runs.test.ts covering no rows, multiple plan-runs for one plan id collapsing to a single entry, and project scoping. Verify: bun test src/db/repos/plan-runs.test.ts and bun run check:all green.

Add a lean listSeedStatuses(deps, projectPath) reader to src/seeds-cli that shells `sd list --format json`, parses via the existing SeedsListEnvelopeSchema, and returns a ReadonlyMap<seedId, status> for every issue (not just scheduled ones). Re-export from src/seeds-cli/index.ts. Unit-test the parse + map build against a stub spawn: mixed open/closed statuses, empty list, and non-zero exit wrapping in SeedsCliError. Verify: bun test src/seeds-cli and bun run check:all green.

Add a pure computeReadyPlans({ plans, seedStatusById, dispatchedPlanIds }) helper (new module under src/plan-runs) where each plan carries { id, name?, status, children: string[] }. Return only plans with status === 'approved' that have at least one open (non-closed) child seed AND are not in dispatchedPlanIds, each annotated with openChildCount. Unit-test the full truth table: approved+open+undispatched (included), approved+all-children-closed (excluded), approved+already-dispatched (excluded), and non-approved (excluded). Verify: bun test for the new module and bun run check:all green.

Add the GET /projects/:id/ready-plans handler in src/server/handlers/projects.ts gated exactly like listProjectSeedPlansHandler (project 404, hasSeeds → 400, seedsCli configured → 400). Orchestrate: listPlans, filter to status 'approved', resolve each approved plan's children via showPlan, build the status map via listSeedStatuses, fetch dispatched ids via repos.planRuns.listDispatchedPlanIds, then return { plans } from computeReadyPlans (entries { id, name?, status, openChildCount }). Register the route in ROUTE_TABLE (src/server/handlers/index.ts) and regenerate docs with bun run gen:docs and bun run gen:openapi. Add handler tests: a ready plan surfaces, an all-children-closed plan is hidden, an already-dispatched plan is hidden, and a non-seeds project returns 400. Verify: bun run check:all green including gen:docs:check and gen:openapi:check.

Add typed client facades for the new endpoint: src/client/client.ts listReadyPlans(projectId) plus its response type in src/client/types.ts, AND the UI facade projectsApi.readyPlans in src/ui/src/api/client.ts plus a ReadyPlan type in src/ui/src/api/types.ts. Mirror the existing seedPlans facade shape (URL building, AbortSignal passthrough). Add a client unit test asserting the request URL and parsed shape. Verify: bun run check:all green and the UI typechecks via bun run build:ui.

Generalize the operator dispatch popup so it can be opened outside a conversation. Extract the form body of src/ui/src/pages/conversation-detail/dispatch-plan-dialog.tsx into a reusable DispatchPlanDialog accepting { projectId, planId (pre-fillable + optionally locked), plotId?: string | null } and drop the required plannerRunId; keep the conversation caller working by routing its existing planner-run hint through the new planId prop. Dispatch still goes over planRunsApi.create (POST /plan-runs) unchanged. Add or adjust UI tests for the generalized component and the conversation caller. Verify: bun run build:ui and bun run check:all green.

Add a 'Ready to dispatch' tab to src/ui/src/pages/PlanRuns.tsx that toggles between the existing plan-runs table and a ready-plans view. For the selected project the view fetches projectsApi.readyPlans (5s refetch) and renders a table (plan id, name, status, open-child count), each row with a one-click Dispatch button opening the generalized DispatchPlanDialog pre-filled with that plan id + project (+ plot id when the project has .plot/). Match the page's loading/empty/error conventions and prompt the operator to pick a project when none is selected (the endpoint is per-project). Add a UI test covering the tab and one-click open. If the UI bundle grows, re-baseline with bun run check:bundle-size --update. Verify: bun run check:all green.

Add a deterministic, idempotent, self-cleaning acceptance scenario at scripts/acceptance/scenarios/NN-ready-to-dispatch-plans.ts following the scripts/acceptance/lib conventions: register a project with .seeds/, create and approve a plan with one open child, assert GET /projects/:id/ready-plans surfaces it with openChildCount of 1; dispatch a plan-run over POST /plan-runs; assert the plan no longer appears in ready-plans (dedup confirmed). Verify: the scenario passes against a live warren+burrow stack and bun run check:all green.

Document the ready-to-dispatch surface: add a SPEC §11.P note describing the operator-gated, read-on-demand model and the approved + has-open-child + not-dispatched criteria, and state explicitly that background auto-dispatch (Approach A) is deferred. Update README.md and CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md only if the surface count or structure they describe changed. Verify: bun run gen:docs:check, bun run check:agents, and bun run lint green, and every referenced path exists.

Run the .claude/commands/release.md flow with a PATCH version bump: find the last release tag and review changes since it, bump the version in package.json and src/index.ts (export const VERSION) in lockstep at the patch level, move CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entries into a new dated version section, update CLAUDE.md/README.md if command counts or structure changed, then commit. Verify: package.json and src/index.ts VERSION agree, CHANGELOG.md has the new section, and bun run check:all is green.

risks: computeReadyPlans needs each approved plan's child ids; sd plan list strips them in the PlanSummary projection. Mitigation: the endpoint resolves children via showPlan per approved plan (approved plans are few), keeping the compute helper pure and shell-outs in the handler.

Resolving open children per plan could fan out many sd show calls. Mitigation: a single sd list --format json builds one status map joined against plan children, so child status costs one shell-out per project regardless of plan count.

'not dispatched' could be read as 'no active plan-run' vs 'no plan-run at all'. Mitigation: this surface uses 'no plan_run row for the plan id' so dispatching moves a plan out of the tab immediately; resume semantics stay a follow-on concern.

Route additions trip gen:docs:check / gen:openapi:check and the UI tab can trip check:bundle-size. Mitigation: the endpoint and UI steps explicitly regenerate docs/openapi and re-baseline the bundle within cap.

Generalizing the conversation dispatch dialog could regress the existing conversation hand-off. Mitigation: the conversation caller is kept working via the new planId prop and covered by adjusted UI tests in the same step.
acceptance: GET /projects/:id/ready-plans returns only plans that are status 'approved', have at least one open child seed, and have no existing plan-run, each annotated with openChildCount; gates mirror the seeds-plans endpoint (404 / hasSeeds 400 / seedsCli 400).

The Plan runs page has a 'Ready to dispatch' tab that lists those plans for the selected project and dispatches a selected plan via a one-click popup over the existing POST /plan-runs path, with model/provider/prompt editable.
Once a plan is dispatched it no longer appears in the ready-to-dispatch surface (dedup verified end-to-end by an acceptance scenario).
No background auto-dispatch is introduced; dispatch remains fully operator-gated.
bun run check:all is fully green (including gen:docs:check, gen:openapi:check, check:bundle-size, and check:coverage) and an acceptance scenario exercises the surface end-to-end.
The final step ships a patch release via the .claude/commands/release.md flow with package.json and src/index.ts VERSION in agreement and a new dated CHANGELOG section.

sections.context: ratchetwatch 2026-06-21 patrol (docs/CONSTITUTION.md Article II — ratchets only tighten). Two mechanical tightenings warranted: (1) coverage functions floor has 1.01pt slack vs actual (89.61% actual / 88.60% floor), above the ~0.75pt debt threshold; lines slack is 0.31pt (91.85% / 91.54%) and is left alone. (2) src/diagnostics/checks.ts is 616 lines — the grandfathered file furthest over the 500-line global limit that is NOT already covered by an open seed (pr.ts/warren-70d7, the four warren-c65d files, and bridge.test.ts/warren-889a are all already tracked). At most one decomposition per patrol per the standing rule.
sections.behavior_invariant: Every quality gate stays green after each step. The raised coverage functions floor (89.36) must still pass against the current measured actual (89.61%). The diagnostics decomposition must change ZERO runtime behavior: checkBwrap/checkCanopyClone/checkCanopyClean/checkWarrenConfig/checkWarrenConfigDeprecations/checkBurrowReachable/checkWarrenDb/checkDatabaseReachable/checkPreviewPortAllocator/checkPreviewMaxLive/checkPreviewAuthStrength/checkBurrowPoolReachable and all exported types/consts (DiagnosticCheck, EnvLike, ExistsFn, WarrenConfigCheckProject, CheckWarrenConfigDeps, PreviewPortUsageProbe, PreviewLiveCountProbe, PREVIEW_TOKEN_PLACEHOLDERS, PREVIEW_MIN_TOKEN_LENGTH, BWRAP_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS, CANOPY_GIT_TIMEOUT_MS) must remain importable from src/diagnostics/checks.ts with identical signatures and identical /readyz + `warren doctor` output. The existing test files (checks.test.ts, checks.config.test.ts, checks.preview.test.ts) must pass unchanged with the same total count.
sections.approach: Step 1 is a one-number JSON edit to the coverage floor (no source change). Step 2 keeps src/diagnostics/checks.ts as a thin BARREL: shared types/consts stay, and the three check groups move into sibling files that checks.ts re-exports — so every importer (src/server/handlers/diagnostics.ts, src/cli/commands/doctor.ts, and the three sibling test files), which all import from ./checks.ts, keeps resolving unchanged. Mirror the existing test-side split seams (checks.test.ts=sandbox/canopy, checks.config.test.ts=config/db, checks.preview.test.ts=preview).

steps.title: Raise coverage functions floor 88.60 -> 89.36 in scripts/coverage-budgets.json
steps.description: In scripts/coverage-budgets.json set "functions": 89.36 (was 88.60; current actual 89.61%, minus 0.25pt margin). Leave "lines": 91.54 UNCHANGED (actual 91.85%, slack 0.31pt is within the ~0.75pt threshold — do not touch it). Update the _comment parenthetical baseline note to record the new measurement: 'functions 89.61%, lines 91.85% as of 2026-06-21, ratchetwatch'. Change nothing else. Per Article II the ratchet only goes UP; this raise must still pass against current actuals. Verify: `bun install --frozen-lockfile` (node_modules required for full coverage) then `bun run check:coverage` exits 0 and prints functions at or above 89.36 and lines at or above 91.54.

steps.title: Decompose src/diagnostics/checks.ts (616 lines) below the 500-line global limit via a barrel re-export
steps.description: src/diagnostics/checks.ts is 616 lines — the grandfathered file furthest over the 500-line limit not already covered by an open seed. Split it into sibling files under src/diagnostics/ and keep checks.ts as a thin barrel. Keep the shared types/consts in checks.ts (interface DiagnosticCheck, type EnvLike, type ExistsFn, BWRAP_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS, CANOPY_GIT_TIMEOUT_MS) and re-export every moved symbol from checks.ts (export ... from "./checks-<group>.ts") so all importers keep resolving via ./checks.ts. Move groups mirroring the existing test split seams: (a) checks-sandbox.ts <- checkBwrap, checkCanopyClone, checkCanopyClean; (b) checks-config.ts <- checkWarrenConfig, checkWarrenConfigDeprecations, checkBurrowReachable, checkWarrenDb, checkDatabaseReachable, checkBurrowPoolReachable plus their exported types (WarrenConfigCheckProject, CheckWarrenConfigDeps); (c) checks-preview.ts <- checkPreviewPortAllocator, checkPreviewMaxLive, checkPreviewAuthStrength plus PreviewPortUsageProbe, PreviewLiveCountProbe, PREVIEW_TOKEN_PLACEHOLDERS, PREVIEW_MIN_TOKEN_LENGTH. Each group file imports the shared types from ./checks.ts (or a tiny checks-types.ts if a cycle appears — keep ONE copy, do NOT duplicate, or jscpd check:dups flags it). Do NOT alter any check body, message, hint, or signature. Then REMOVE the "src/diagnostics/checks.ts": 616 entry from scripts/file-size-budgets.json and do NOT add budget entries for the new sibling files (they must default-pass under the 500-line threshold — confirm `wc -l` for each < 500). Per docs/CONSTITUTION.md Article VI, before declaring done run a repo-wide search for the old path across ALL file types — `grep -rn "diagnostics/checks" --include='*' . | grep -v node_modules` (or `git grep -n "diagnostics/checks"`) PLUS an explicit sweep of Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, .github/workflows/*.yml, src/supervisor/ spawn/config strings, scripts/acceptance/, and docs/ — fix any stale reference (file moves have broken production here before; encode the check, do not assume it). Note the known importers that MUST stay green: src/server/handlers/diagnostics.ts and src/cli/commands/doctor.ts (both import named check fns + DiagnosticCheck/WarrenConfigCheckProject from ../../diagnostics/checks.ts) and the three sibling test files. Verify: each of `wc -l src/diagnostics/checks.ts src/diagnostics/checks-sandbox.ts src/diagnostics/checks-config.ts src/diagnostics/checks-preview.ts` shows < 500 AND `bun test src/diagnostics/` reports the same pass/0-fail total as before AND `bun run typecheck` is clean AND `bun run check:size` exits 0 AND `bun run check:dups` exits 0 AND `bun run check:all` is fully green.

acceptance: scripts/coverage-budgets.json functions floor is 89.36 and `bun run check:coverage` exits 0 with actuals at or above the floors (functions >= 89.36, lines >= 91.54).

src/diagnostics/checks.ts and every new sibling (checks-sandbox.ts, checks-config.ts, checks-preview.ts) are each < 500 lines and the 616-line entry is removed from scripts/file-size-budgets.json with no new entries added.

All diagnostics tests pass unchanged (same total) and `bun run typecheck`, `bun run check:size`, `bun run check:dups` exit 0 — /readyz and `warren doctor` output is byte-identical (no check body/message/hint/signature changed).
Article VI: a repo-wide search for the old path (incl. Dockerfile, workflow YAML, supervisor/config strings, scripts/acceptance, docs) finds no stale reference; all importers resolve via ./checks.ts.
`bun run check:all` is fully green — every gate stays green and no ratchet was loosened.

name: Cluster C burndown: self-contained bugs + chores

sections.context: Patrol scan of warren on 2026-06-22. Quality gates (bun test 2979 pass, bun run lint, bun run typecheck) are all green, so this patrol targets correctness/consistency issues the linter cannot see. All findings cluster in the freshly-landed observability instrumentation (commit 29c09f4a, 'Add observability instrumentation: Sentry + Prometheus Phase 1'): a defense-in-depth secret-scrub gap, a Prometheus metric-naming convention violation, and a readonly-array mutation via double-cast. No feature work, no API-signature changes, no dependency changes.
sections.behavior_invariant: The /metrics endpoint must keep emitting the same set of run-state, cost, token, bridge, and log-rate series (only metric NAMES change for the _total fix); the Prometheus exposition must stay valid v0.0.4 text. The Sentry scrubber must keep redacting every secret-shaped field it already redacts at the top level (existing tests stay green) and additionally redact nested ones. MetricsRegistry accumulation/sorting semantics and the no-op-tracker behavior when SENTRY_DSN is unset must be byte-identical.
sections.approach:
Three small, independently-landable fixes, each with a unit test pinning the corrected behavior. Land the secret-scrub fix first (independent file), then the two metrics.ts fixes serially to avoid same-file merge churn, then a release because two of the three changes are consumer-observable.

FINDING 1 (security + parity + doc drift) - src/observability/error-tracking.ts scrubRecord() ~lines 116-123 and scrubSentryEvent() ~lines 98-110. scrubRecord only redacts TOP-LEVEL keys, but src/observability/log-sink.ts forwardLogToSinks() (~lines 56-66) forwards the raw structured pino log object to Sentry as extra BEFORE pino's serialize-time redact runs, and the central policy in src/server/main/redact.ts (LOG_REDACT_PATHS) explicitly censors one level of nesting via the *.<field> wildcard. So a nested secret like { extra: { config: { token } } } leaks into Sentry's extra even though the same field is censored in the log line, and the module doc comment ('even if a caller attaches a whole config/headers object as context') is false. Fix: make scrubRecord recurse into nested record values one level (matching the pino *.<field> policy); keep top-level behavior identical.

FINDING 2 (consistency / Prometheus naming convention) - src/server/handlers/metrics.ts ~lines 47-56. warren_cost_usd_total, warren_tokens_input_total, warren_tokens_output_total are type:'gauge' but carry the _total suffix Prometheus reserves for counters (promlint: 'non-counter metrics should not have _total suffix'). They are live SUMs over src/db/repos/runs-stats.ts aggregateRunCost and can decrease when runs are reaped, so gauge is correct - drop the _total suffix (warren_cost_usd, warren_tokens_input, warren_tokens_output). The genuine counter warren_log_messages_total keeps its suffix. Update src/server/handlers/metrics.test.ts line ~92.

FINDING 3 (type safety) - src/server/handlers/metrics.ts countersToMetrics() ~lines 73-90 pushes onto existing.samples via a double-cast (existing.samples as {...}[]).push(sample), mutating an array typed readonly PromSample[]. Fix: accumulate samples in a mutable array keyed by metric name, then build each PromMetric once - no cast, no readonly mutation.

steps.title: Deepen Sentry event scrubber to match the pino redact nesting policy
Fix Prometheus _total suffix on gauge metrics in /metrics
Remove readonly-array mutation double-cast in countersToMetrics
Release: run /release per .claude/commands/release.md

acceptance: Step 1: a new unit test in src/observability/error-tracking.test.ts asserts scrubSentryEvent redacts a secret nested one level deep in event.extra (e.g. { extra: { config: { token: 'x' } } } -> token '[Redacted]') and in event.request.headers, while the three existing top-level scrub tests still pass. The module doc comment claiming protection 'even if a caller attaches a whole config/headers object as context' is now true.

Step 2: GET /metrics no longer emits a gauge whose name ends in _total; src/server/handlers/metrics.test.ts asserts the renamed cost/token gauges (e.g. warren_cost_usd, warren_tokens_input, warren_tokens_output) and that warren_log_messages_total (a real counter) keeps its suffix. bun run gen:docs:check / gen:openapi:check stay green.

Step 3: countersToMetrics builds its PromMetric samples without an `as {...}[]` cast onto a readonly array; bun run typecheck stays clean and the metrics handler test still renders multi-series counters correctly.
Whole plan: bun run check:all is fully green (lint, typecheck, tests, coverage, dups, size, debt, docs, openapi).

sections.approach: Three decomposition PRs carried a noExcessiveCognitiveComplexity:off exemption into a newborn sibling file instead of refactoring the moved function under cognitive-complexity 15. For each file, simplify the offending function (extract helpers / flatten branches) until it passes the gate, then delete its entry from the biome.json complexity override includes block. The ratchet only goes down. Verify with `bun run lint` (which runs biome --error-on-warnings) after each removal; the file must pass with no override.
sections.behavior_invariant: No runtime behavior changes. Each refactored function keeps identical inputs, outputs, and side effects; only internal structure changes. `bun test` and `bun run typecheck` stay green; public export surfaces unchanged.

sections.context: Nightwatch patrol of 2026-06-23. Quality gates are green: `bun run lint`, `bun run typecheck`, `bun run check:debt`, and `bun run check:dups` all pass; no `as any`, `@ts-ignore`, or untracked debt markers exist in non-test `src/`. The patrol found one concrete defect: a non-deterministic test in `src/runs/spawn/plot-append.test.ts` that fails whenever the host environment defines `WARREN_API_TOKEN` / `WARREN_API_URL` (which is exactly the case inside a deployed warren container and in CI sandboxes). The test `omits env from the burrow up call when no plotId is set (warren-e26f)` calls `spawnRun(...)` without passing `serverEnv`, so dispatch falls back to `process.env` (`src/runs/spawn/dispatch.ts:441` — `serverEnv ?? process.env`). `injectWarrenCallbackEnv` (`src/runs/spawn/callback-env.ts`) then copies the ambient `WARREN_API_TOKEN` and a derived `WARREN_API_URL` into the burrow up-call env. The test asserts `expect(env).toEqual({ BUN_INSTALL_CACHE_DIR: "/tmp/bun-install-cache" })`, which fails because the env now also contains the leaked callback vars. Verified: with `env -u WARREN_API_TOKEN -u WARREN_API_URL bun test` the file is 10/10 green; with those vars set it is 9/10. This is a test-isolation defect, not a product bug — the token injection itself is intended behavior (warren-f248). No source/behavior change is required.
sections.behavior_invariant: The production behavior of `spawnRun` and `injectWarrenCallbackEnv` MUST remain byte-identical. The only change is to make the test deterministic by supplying an explicit `serverEnv` so the assertion no longer depends on the ambient process environment. After the fix, `src/runs/spawn/plot-append.test.ts` must pass both with and without `WARREN_API_TOKEN` / `WARREN_API_URL` present in the environment, and all other spawn tests must remain green.
sections.approach: Make the env-sensitive test(s) in `src/runs/spawn/plot-append.test.ts` deterministic by passing an explicit empty `serverEnv: {}` to the `spawnRun(...)` calls that assert on the burrow up-call env, so the callback-env injection has no ambient token to copy. Update the now-inaccurate inline comment (line ~100: 'env is always set (at minimum BUN_INSTALL_CACHE_DIR — warren-b893)') to note that callback env (WARREN_API_TOKEN/WARREN_API_URL) is also injected from `serverEnv` in production and is intentionally suppressed here via an empty `serverEnv`. Do not alter `callback-env.ts`, `dispatch.ts`, or any production code, and do not change public signatures.

sections.context: On the deployed instance (warren-deployed.fly.dev, project prj_z07y6ssfjfpk) the scheduler emits scheduler.cron_failed every ~60s with reason 'spawnRun failed: agent not found: warden-digest'. A weekly cron ('0 5 * * 0') is retrying every single tick instead of once per slot, producing continuous log spam and wasted scheduler ticks that mask real signal. Operators running warren without the canopy audit agents registered (CANOPY_REPO_URL unset) hit this for every audit trigger in .warren/triggers.yaml (warden-digest, nightwatch, bugwatch, gatewatch, ratchetwatch, tastewatch). The fix restores correct no-catch-up cron semantics and quiets the noise so the observability surface stays trustworthy.
sections.reproduction:
1. Run warren with a project whose .warren/triggers.yaml has a cron entry (e.g. cron '0 5 * * 0') referencing an agent name that is NOT in the agent registry (no inline built-in, no canopy library entry).
2. Let the scheduler tick (default 60s) past the cron's first elapsed slot.
Observed: scheduler.cron_failed fires every ~60s with reason 'spawnRun failed: agent not found: <role>' — the same elapsed slot is re-dispatched on every tick because the trigger row's lastFiredAt never advances on the failed dispatch.
Expected: the slot is consumed once; the operator sees at most one warning per cron slot and the scheduler does not retry the unregistered agent every tick.
sections.root_cause: In src/triggers/dispatch.ts, dispatchCronTrigger fires when parsed.cron.previousRun(now) > row.lastFiredAt. When the spawn call throws, the catch block returns { kind: 'error' } WITHOUT calling repos.triggers.recordFire / upsert, so lastFiredAt is never advanced (see the comment 'Next tick will recompute prev > last and retry'). For a transient failure (burrow briefly down) retrying is correct, but for a PERMANENT failure — spawnRun throws NotFoundError 'agent not found: <agentName>' from src/runs/spawn/dispatch.ts when repos.agents.resolve returns null — the condition prev > last stays true for the entire slot window (a full week for '0 5 * * 0'), so every tick re-attempts and re-fails. The defect is that the dispatcher treats a deterministic, won't-resolve-this-slot failure identically to a transient one.
sections.approach: Distinguish permanent spawn failures from transient ones in dispatchCronTrigger. When the spawn error is an agent-not-found (NotFoundError with the 'agent not found:' shape), consume the cron slot by advancing the trigger row's lastFiredAt+nextFireAt via the existing repos.triggers.upsert (no runId — the existing UpsertTriggerInput already supports lastFiredAt/nextFireAt without lastRunId) and return a distinct skipped/error result carrying the reason. This way the elapsed slot is recorded as handled, so the next tick sees prev <= last and does NOT retry until the next genuine slot — i.e. warn-once-per-slot instead of every-tick spam, while preserving no-catch-up semantics. Transient failures keep the current retry-next-tick behavior (no row advance). Chosen over fix-option (3) 'validate trigger roles at config load' because that is broader (config-load surface, HTTP/UI error plumbing) and over fix-option (1) 'just register the agents' because that is an operator action, not a code fix — the scheduler should be robust to unregistered roles regardless. No public API, dependency, or architectural changes; the change is local to the triggers module.

steps.title: Classify permanent agent-not-found spawn failures in dispatchCronTrigger
Consume the cron slot on permanent failure to stop every-tick retry
Add regression tests for permanent-vs-transient cron dispatch failure
Release: run /release per .claude/commands/release.md

acceptance: src/triggers/dispatch.ts: when spawn throws an 'agent not found' NotFoundError, dispatchCronTrigger advances the trigger row (lastFiredAt + nextFireAt) so the elapsed slot is consumed.
Regression test: two consecutive ticks across one elapsed cron slot with an unregistered agent dispatch the spawn at most ONCE (not once per tick); the second tick returns the no-new-slot skip.
Regression test: a transient (non-NotFoundError) spawn failure does NOT advance lastFiredAt and is retried on the next tick (existing behavior preserved).
scheduler.cron_failed log noise drops from every-tick to at most once per cron slot for an unregistered-agent trigger.
bun test src/triggers/, bun run lint, and bun run typecheck all pass.

name: Stop every-tick cron retry on permanent agent-not-found

sections.context:
Code-patrol scan of 2026-06-25. The repo is heavily linted and patrolled (lint + typecheck both clean; no `as any`, no loose-equality bugs, no missing parseInt radix). The scan surfaced two low-risk, isolated issues, both in src/triggers/dispatch.ts: (1) an unreachable instanceof branch in the local formatError helper (dead code), and (2) a user-facing dispatch error message that points operators at the deprecated `.warren/defaults.json` config file instead of the canonical `.warren/config.yaml`. Both are internal hygiene / message-text corrections — no behavior, API, security, or performance surface changes.

Finding 1 (dead code): src/triggers/dispatch.ts lines 329-330. `formatError` checks `if (err instanceof Error) return err.message;` (329) then `if (err instanceof TriggerDispatchError) return err.message;` (330). Since TriggerDispatchError extends WarrenError extends Error (src/core/errors.ts), the Error check on 329 always wins, so 330 is unreachable and returns an identical value. Fix: delete line 330 and the now-unused `import { TriggerDispatchError } from "./errors.ts";` on line 35 (lint will flag the unused import otherwise).

Finding 2 (message drift): src/triggers/dispatch.ts line 253, in dispatchScheduledSeed: `reason: "no agent to dispatch — set defaults.defaultRole in .warren/defaults.json"`. `.warren/defaults.json` is the deprecated legacy config (CLAUDE.md; warren-config/load.ts emits a deprecation warning for it); the canonical home is `.warren/config.yaml`. Every other user-facing message (cli/commands/init.ts, config-migrate.ts, diagnostics/checks-config.ts) already points at config.yaml / `warren config migrate`. Fix: reword the reason to reference setting `defaultRole` in `.warren/config.yaml`, matching the surrounding messaging.
sections.behavior_invariant: The dispatch decision logic and result discrimination are unchanged. dispatchScheduledSeed still returns {kind:'error'} (exact same kind, no spawn) when no default role is configured; dispatchCronTrigger's fire/seed/skip/error outcomes and slot-consumption semantics are byte-identical. formatError still returns err.message for every Error subclass (including TriggerDispatchError and NotFoundError) and String(err) for non-Errors — only the now-unreachable second branch is removed. All existing tests in src/triggers/dispatch.test.ts and dispatch-scheduled.test.ts continue to pass without modification (none assert the exact message string).
sections.approach: Two small, independent, single-file edits in src/triggers/dispatch.ts. No public signatures change, no new APIs, no dependency changes. Each lands as its own PR.

steps.title: Remove unreachable TriggerDispatchError branch in dispatch.ts formatError (line 330) and its now-unused import
Point dispatchScheduledSeed no-role error at .warren/config.yaml, not deprecated defaults.json

acceptance: bun run lint and bun run typecheck both stay green.
bun test src/triggers/ passes unchanged (no test edits required).
src/triggers/dispatch.ts formatError contains exactly two branches: `if (err instanceof Error) return err.message;` then `return String(err);` — the TriggerDispatchError branch is gone.

The dispatchScheduledSeed no-role error reason references `.warren/config.yaml` (the canonical path) and the `defaultRole` key, consistent with init.ts / config-migrate.ts / diagnostics messaging; no remaining user-facing error message points solely at `.warren/defaults.json`.

sections.approach: Single mechanical grandfather-decomposition this patrol (docs/CONSTITUTION.md Article II burn-down, one file per patrol). Coverage is within slack (functions 89.88% / lines 91.98%, both under 0.75pt over their floors), bundle creep is ~1.5KB gzip over 7d (under threshold), the debt-marker allowlist is empty, and src/runs/pr.ts (361 lines, now under the 500 limit) removal is already tracked by warren-70d7 — so none of those warrant a step here. The one tightening is decomposing scripts/acceptance/scenarios/26-plan-run-roundtrip.ts (563 lines, +63 over the 500 global limit, furthest-over grandfathered file not under an open seed) by extracting its helper group into a sibling .helpers.ts, mirroring the established precedent scripts/acceptance/scenarios/20-preview.helpers.ts (warren-65f6) and 32-plot-workbench-loop.helpers.ts, then removing its budget entry. No release step (Article III: hygiene batches into the next real release).
sections.behavior_invariant: The scenario's runtime behavior is unchanged: scripts/acceptance/run.ts still imports `scenario as scenario26` from ./scenarios/26-plan-run-roundtrip.ts, the `scenario` export and its acceptance logic are byte-identical, and the full check suite (typecheck, check:size, check:dups, check:all) stays green. Only the physical location of file-local helpers changes.

steps.title: Extract the helper group from scripts/acceptance/scenarios/26-plan-run-roundtrip.ts into a new sibling scripts/acceptance/scenarios/26-plan-run-roundtrip.helpers.ts, mirroring the precedent scripts/acceptance/scenarios/20-preview.helpers.ts (warren-65f6) and 32-plot-workbench-loop.helpers.ts. MOVE out of the scenario file into the new helpers file every file-local symbol OTHER THAN the `export const scenario` body (lines ~118-336) and its imports: the row/response interfaces ProjectRow, RunRow, PlanRunRow, PlanRunChildRow, CreatePlanRunResponse, PlanRunDetailResponse, EventRow (lines ~49-105); the module constants PLAN_PROJECT_URL, PLAN_ID, SEED_A, SEED_B, SEED_C, SEED_TS, TERMINAL_PLAN_STATES, PLAN_DEADLINE_MS, POLL_INTERVAL_MS (lines ~107-116); and the helper types/functions BuildPlanRunFixtureInput, buildPlanRunFixture, seedRowOpen, seedRowClosed, planRow, rewriteSourceSeedClosed, waitForPlanState, fetchAllPlanRunEvents, RunResult, runIn, withGitIdentity, sleep (lines ~337-563). EXPORT every moved symbol from the helpers file. Carry into the helpers file ONLY the imports those moved symbols use (from node:fs, node:fs/promises, node:os, node:path, ../lib/assert.ts, ../lib/http.ts WarrenHttp, ../lib/inproc.ts) — keep ONE copy of any shared helper, do NOT duplicate (jscpd check:dups will flag copy-paste). In 26-plan-run-roundtrip.ts, add an import of the symbols the scenario body still references back from ./26-plan-run-roundtrip.helpers.ts (at minimum: buildPlanRunFixture, fetchAllPlanRunEvents, rewriteSourceSeedClosed, waitForPlanState, ProjectRow, CreatePlanRunResponse, PLAN_PROJECT_URL, PLAN_ID, PLAN_DEADLINE_MS, SEED_A, SEED_B, SEED_C — typecheck will flag any other missing/unused import); keep the `export const scenario` body, the header comment block, and the `scenario as scenario26` import contract in run.ts unchanged. Do NOT alter any assertion, fixture string, or scenario logic. Do NOT add a budget entry for the new helpers file — it must default-pass under the 500-line threshold. Verify: `wc -l scripts/acceptance/scenarios/26-plan-run-roundtrip.ts scripts/acceptance/scenarios/26-plan-run-roundtrip.helpers.ts` each shows < 500 AND `bun run typecheck` is clean AND `bun run check:dups` exits 0.

Remove the "scripts/acceptance/scenarios/26-plan-run-roundtrip.ts": 563 entry from scripts/file-size-budgets.json (delete that single line). Do NOT add a budget entry for the new sibling 26-plan-run-roundtrip.helpers.ts — it must default-pass under the 500-line threshold (confirm `wc -l scripts/acceptance/scenarios/26-plan-run-roundtrip.helpers.ts` shows < 500). Per docs/CONSTITUTION.md Article VI (refactors prove runtime paths), before declaring done run a repo-wide search for the old single-file assumption AND the new path across ALL file types — `rg -n "26-plan-run-roundtrip" --hidden -g '!node_modules' .` (or `git grep -n 26-plan-run-roundtrip`) — PLUS an explicit sweep of scripts/acceptance/run.ts, Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, .github/workflows/*.yml, src/supervisor/ spawn/config strings, and docs/ — and fix any stale reference (file moves have broken production here before; encode the check, do not assume it; note run.ts imports `scenario as scenario26` and that import must still resolve). Verify: `bun run check:size` exits 0 AND `bun run check:dups` exits 0 AND `bun run check:all` is fully green (every gate stays green; both resulting files default-pass under the 500-line threshold).

acceptance: scripts/acceptance/scenarios/26-plan-run-roundtrip.ts and scripts/acceptance/scenarios/26-plan-run-roundtrip.helpers.ts are each < 500 lines (wc -l).
The "scripts/acceptance/scenarios/26-plan-run-roundtrip.ts": 563 entry is gone from scripts/file-size-budgets.json and no entry was added for the new helpers file.
scripts/acceptance/run.ts still imports `scenario as scenario26` from ./scenarios/26-plan-run-roundtrip.ts and bun run typecheck is clean.
Article VI repo-wide search for 26-plan-run-roundtrip (including Dockerfile, workflow YAML, supervisor/config strings, docs) shows no stale path references.
bun run check:all is fully green — check:size, check:dups, typecheck, lint, and coverage all pass against current actuals.
sections.context: docs/CONSTITUTION.md Article II requires grandfather lists to burn down, not just hold. scripts/file-size-budgets.json grandfathers 13 oversized files against the 500-line global limit (scripts/check-size.ts). This patrol's burn-down target is scripts/acceptance/scenarios/26-plan-run-roundtrip.ts at 563 lines (+63 over limit) — the furthest-over entry not already covered by an open seed (src/runs/pr.ts removal is tracked by warren-70d7). The file is an acceptance scenario whose runtime contract is a single `scenario` export consumed by scripts/acceptance/run.ts (`import { scenario as scenario26 }`); the bulk of its lines are file-local fixtures/helpers (interfaces, constants, buildPlanRunFixture, waitForPlanState, etc.) that extract cleanly into a sibling .helpers.ts, exactly as warren-65f6 did for 20-preview.ts. Article VI applies: an acceptance-scenario path is referenced by run.ts and potentially docs, so a repo-wide old-path search (including non-source files) is mandatory before declaring done.

Warren has exactly four open GitHub issues and all four are confirmed real against main via subagent code inspection. #420 (bug): the reap bookkeeping helpers run a bare `git commit` with no pathspec, so any unrelated pre-staged files get swept into a mislabeled, hook-bypassing chore(warren) commit (observed downstream in kessellab/kessel). #487 (P1 bug): the plan-run auto-done Plot transition is dead in the default flow because nothing promotes a Plot from ready->active, so the SPEC 11.P.Plot documented auto-done never fires (it only skips). #486 (P2 bug): the UI hardcodes `.warren/defaults.json` even when config loads from config.yaml, showing wrong info to operators. #419 (feature): runs cannot target an existing branch and have reap push back to it, so repair/review/verify loops create a new branch instead of updating the one they were dispatched from. This plan closes all four in a single coordinated batch converging on one release-prep step.
sections.approach: Forward-chained, single-agent-sized child seeds, grouped by issue, converging on one terminal release-prep seed. #420 is a self-contained one-file-plus-test fix using `git commit --only -- <pathspecs>`. #487 uses promotion-at-dispatch (promote ready->active via a dedicated fire-and-log seam, leaving the auto-done guard ===active unchanged) because the issue's 'simpler' option of widening the guard is broken by plot-cli's illegal ready->done transition; it splits into a code-seam+wiring seed and a SPEC/acceptance reconcile seed. #486 uses a dynamic sourceFile field threaded loader->API->UI (not a static string swap, which would lie for legacy installs), split server-side then UI-side. #419 uses Option B (minimal reconcile, per maintainer decision): keep today's branch-override semantics and the untouched CI-fixer, and only add the missing pieces -- persist a new runs.target_branch column, expose it via POST /runs, and surface it on the wire types -- because reap already pushes to the target (the burrow branch is the target). Schema/migration lands first, then dispatch/spawn wiring and wire types fan out from it. A final seed bumps VERSION in both files plus CHANGELOG and runs the full gate suite.

alternatives.name: #419 Option A (fork design: unique burrow branch + targetBranch forwarded as burrow baseBranch fork-point + reap pushes HEAD:<targetBranch> + new persisted column)
alternatives.rejected_because: Per maintainer decision: it changes the existing CI-fixer targetBranch semantics, forces re-pointing scheduler.ts/poller.ts plus composeRunBranch tests, and is a larger blast radius. Chose Option B (minimal reconcile).

alternatives.name: #487 widen the auto-done guard to also accept `ready`
alternatives.rejected_because: @os-eco/plot-cli rejects the illegal ready->done transition, turning skips into plan_run.plot_auto_done_failed; promoting ready->active at dispatch respects the drafting->ready->active->done lifecycle and keeps the auto-done guard ===active.

alternatives.name: #486 static string swap to `.warren/config.yaml` everywhere
alternatives.rejected_because: Legacy installs that still load defaults.json would then have the UI lie in the other direction; a dynamic sourceFile field stays accurate for both.

alternatives.name: #420 leave the bare `git commit`
alternatives.rejected_because: It sweeps unrelated staged files into a mislabeled, hook-bypassing chore(warren) commit (observed downstream in kessellab/kessel).

steps.title: #420 reap: path-limit bookkeeping commits with `git commit --only`. In src/runs/reap/stage.ts append `--only -- .plot/` to the stagePlotForCommit commit argv and `--only -- .seeds/issues.jsonl .seeds/plans.jsonl` (derive from SEEDS_COMMITTABLE_FILES) to stageSeedsForCommit; narrow the seeds staged-delta guard (git diff --cached --quiet) to those two pathspecs for symmetry. Update the two existing toEqual commit-arg assertions and add two regression tests in src/runs/reap/stage.test.ts asserting pre-staged unrelated files are NOT swept into the chore(warren) commits. Run bun test + bun run check:all.

#487 plan-run: add the ready->active Plot promotion seam and wire it into dispatch. In src/plan-runs/plot-appender.ts add PlanRunPlotActivator + defaultPlanRunPlotActivator (open UserPlotClient, read(), if status===ready setStatus(active) else skip) + a fire-and-log promotePlotToActiveOnDispatch wrapper logging plan_run.plot_activated/_skipped/_failed; re-export from src/plan-runs/index.ts; add an optional planRunPlotActivator seam to ServerDeps in src/server/types.ts; call it under the existing plotId!==null guard right after emitPlanRunDispatchedToPlot in BOTH src/server/handlers/plan-runs.ts and src/server/handlers/plot-plan-runs.ts. Add plot-appender activation unit tests + handler tests. Leave the auto-done guard ===active unchanged.

#487 docs/acceptance: reconcile SPEC 11.P.Plot with the dispatch-time ready->active promotion (add the promotion node to the data-flow, note it in the plan_run_dispatched section, and clarify the auto-done guard reaches active via operator OR dispatch promotion) and flip acceptance scenarios 27 and 31 to initialize the bound Plot at `ready` and assert dispatch promotes it to `active` then the final child merge flips it to `done`. Update scripts/acceptance/scenarios accordingly. No version bump here (done in the release step).

#486 config: surface the loaded config source file from the loader through the API. Add `sourceFile: string | null` to LoadedWarrenConfig in src/warren-config/load.ts (= .warren/config.yaml when config.yaml exists, else .warren/defaults.json when only the legacy file exists, else null), set it on both the bootstrap early-return and the final return; thread loaded.sourceFile into the GET /projects/:id/warren-config response in src/server/handlers/projects.ts. Update src/warren-config/load.test.ts and src/server/handlers/projects.warren-config.test.ts assertions.

#486 ui: render the dynamic config source file. Add `sourceFile: string | null` to WarrenConfigResponse in src/ui/src/api/types.ts; in src/ui/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx thread sourceFile into DefaultsBlock and render `{sourceFile ?? '.warren/config.yaml'}` at the hardcoded label (line ~348); in src/ui/src/pages/NewRun.tsx derive configSourceFile = warrenConfig.data?.sourceFile ?? '.warren/config.yaml' and replace the four hardcoded `.warren/defaults.json` UI strings (lines ~302/402/433/460) plus update the two stale comments (108/147). Re-baseline bundle size only if the ratchet flags it.

#419 schema: add nullable runs.target_branch column + generated migrations + repo persistence (Option B). Add `targetBranch: text("target_branch")` to the runs table in BOTH src/db/schema/sqlite.ts and src/db/schema/postgres.ts (after pr_url); run `bun run db:generate` and commit the generated sqlite (0027_*) + postgres (0021_*) migrations, snapshots, and journal entries; add `targetBranch?: string | null` to CreateRunInput and `targetBranch: input.targetBranch ?? null` to the create() row literal in src/db/repos/runs.ts. Ensure drift.test.ts + migrations.test.ts + typecheck pass.

#419 dispatch+spawn: persist and honor targetBranch (Option B, keep current branch-override semantics). In src/server/handlers/runs/dispatch.ts parse `targetBranch = optionalString(body, 'targetBranch')` and pass it into the spawnRun options; in src/runs/spawn/dispatch.ts persist input.targetBranch on runs.create and default the root-run base ref to `input.ref ?? input.targetBranch`. Keep the composeRunBranch override and leave the CI-fixer (scheduler.ts/poller.ts) untouched; reap needs no change because the burrow branch equals targetBranch. Add POST /runs handler tests + spawn refresh/persistence tests.

#419 wire types: expose targetBranch on the client + UI types. Add `targetBranch: string | null` to RunRow and `targetBranch?: string` to CreateRunInput and DispatchRunInput in src/client/types.ts; add `if (input.targetBranch !== undefined) body.targetBranch = input.targetBranch` to the dispatch body builder in src/client/client.ts; add the matching fields to RunRow + CreateRunInput in src/ui/src/api/types.ts; update src/client/client.runs.test.ts to assert the mapping.

Release prep for the four-issue batch: add CHANGELOG.md entries for #420/#487/#486/#419, bump VERSION in BOTH package.json and src/index.ts (kept in sync per the release workflow), run gen:docs:check + gen:openapi:check (regen with gen:docs/gen:openapi only if flagged), and run a full `bun run check:all` (incl. coverage ratchet and ci-postgres-relevant migration parity). Verify all four GitHub issues are addressed end-to-end, then close the seeds.

risks: #487: ready->done is illegal in plot-cli; the promotion must hop ready->active only and the auto-done guard must stay ===active.

#487: two dispatch entry points (plan-runs.ts + plot-plan-runs.ts) must both promote or SPEC 11.Q regresses; promotion must be fire-and-log so it never fails POST /plan-runs; verify the host-side .plot/ status write survives the reap snapshot/restore path.

#419: adding the schema column makes targetBranch a required key in the RunsRepo.create RunRow literal -- typecheck fails until updated; both sqlite + postgres migrations must be generated by db:generate (not hand-written) and ci-postgres.yml runs the live pg migrator.
#420: the seeds staged-delta guard checks all of .seeds/ but the commit is limited to issues.jsonl + plans.jsonl -- narrow the guard to the same pathspecs to avoid an empty-commit throw edge case.

#486: the API response shape change is permissive in V1 (no openapi/docs regen expected) but verify gen:openapi:check / gen:docs:check; the UI string change may nudge the bundle-size ratchet (re-baseline within cap).
acceptance: #420: a reap with unrelated pre-staged files leaves them uncommitted; the chore(warren) plot/seeds commits contain only their pathspecs; regression tests assert this.

#487: dispatching a plan-run against a Plot at `ready` promotes it to `active`, and when the final child PR merges the Plot auto-transitions to `done` (no plan_run.plot_status_skipped); SPEC 11.P.Plot and acceptance scenarios 27/31 are updated.
#486: a project with only .warren/config.yaml shows `.warren/config.yaml` in ProjectDetail + NewRun; a legacy-only project shows `.warren/defaults.json`; loader + handler tests assert sourceFile.

#419: POST /runs accepts targetBranch, persists runs.target_branch, and dispatches a run whose reap pushes back to that branch; client + UI types expose the field; sqlite + postgres migrations present with drift/migrations tests passing.
VERSION is bumped in both package.json and src/index.ts (in sync), CHANGELOG.md has entries for all four issues, and a full `bun run check:all` passes.

name: Close out the four open GitHub issues
